item.aggregated_output:
[bd prime] If this output is truncated by your host, read the full persisted hook output before continuing; it may contain project memories and session rules not visible in the preview.

# Beads Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `bd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session
> Hooks auto-call this in Claude Code when a beads workspace is resolved

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] 1. git status              (check what changed)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage code changes)
[ ] 3. git commit -m "..."     (commit code)
[ ] 4. git push                (push to remote)
```

**NEVER skip this.** Work is not done until pushed.

## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`.
- Persistence you don't need beats lost context
- Git workflow: beads auto-commit to Dolt, run `git push` at session end
- Session management: check `bd ready` for available work

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `bd list --status=open` - All open issues
- `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work
- `bd show <id>` - Detailed issue view with dependencies

### Creating & Updating
- `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low"
- `bd update <id> --claim` - Claim work
- `bd update <id> --assignee=username` - Assign to someone
- `bd update <id> --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline
- `bd close <id>` - Mark complete
- `bd close <id1> <id2> ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient)
- `bd close <id> --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason
- **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency
- **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents

### Dependencies & Blocking
- `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on)
- `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues
- `bd show <id>` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue

### Sync & Collaboration
- `bd dolt push` - Push beads to Dolt remote
- `bd dolt pull` - Pull beads from Dolt remote
- `bd search <query>` - Search issues by keyword

### Project Health
- `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts)
- `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks)
- `bd doctor --check=conventions` - Check for convention drift (lint, stale, orphans)

### Quality Tools
- `bd create --validate` - Check description has required sections
- `bd create --acceptance="criteria"` - Set acceptance criteria (checked by --validate)
- `bd create --design="decisions"` - Record design decisions
- `bd create --notes="context"` - Add supplementary notes
- `bd config set validation.on-create warn` - Auto-validate on every create
- `bd lint` - Check existing issues for missing sections

### Lifecycle & Hygiene
- `bd defer <id> --until="date"` - Defer work to a future date
- `bd supersede <id> --with=<new-id>` - Mark issue as superseded
- `bd close <id> --suggest-next` - Show newly unblocked issues after closing
- `bd stale` - Find issues with no recent activity
- `bd orphans` - Find issues with broken dependencies
- `bd preflight` - Pre-PR checks (lint, stale, orphans)
- `bd human <id>` - Flag for human decision (list/respond/dismiss)

### Structured Workflows
- `bd formula list` - See available workflow templates
- `bd mol pour <name>` - Start structured workflow from formula

## Common Workflows

**Starting work:**
```bash
bd ready           # Find available work
bd show <id>       # Review issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim it
```

**Completing work:**
```bash
bd close <id1> <id2> ...    # Close all completed issues at once
git add . && git commit -m "..."  # Commit code changes
git push                    # Push to remote
```

**Creating dependent work:**
```bash
# Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items)
bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature
bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task
bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx  # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests)
```

diff --git a/tools/registry/verdict-gen.py b/tools/registry/verdict-gen.py
index 270d576e..2ad61ca1 100644
--- a/tools/registry/verdict-gen.py
+++ b/tools/registry/verdict-gen.py
@@ -268,6 +268,65 @@ def apply_amendment_overlay(root, exp_id, record, log_records):
     return effective, applied, kc.frozen_hash(effective)
 
 
+# Scale-language license total order for the min-rule (G-12 vocabulary is the
+# established one across all 17 issued verdicts: none / sign-only / slope).
+SCALE_LICENSE_ORDER = {"none": 0, "sign-only": 1, "slope": 2}
+
+
+def scale_language_license(design, rungs, analysis):
+    """Compute scale_language_licensed with per-record slope enforcement.
+
+    The G-12 count map (docs/research-plan/02-data-and-reporting.md:664) is
+    UNCHANGED: 1 rung -> "none", 2 -> "sign-only", >=3 -> "slope"-ELIGIBLE.
+    What changed (a5-llm Gate-A re-audit REFUTE 2026-07-11, adjudicated in
+    docs/next/a5-llm-refute-adjudication.md section 2.1/2.2, maintainer-approved
+    2026-07-11 issue #5; correction record
+    registry/corrections/a5-llm/2-scale-license-erratum.json): a raw distinct-
+    rung-LABEL count used to license "slope" gate-blind and envelope-blind —
+    for a5-llm it counted the R0 baseline label as a rung and emitted "slope"
+    against a frozen envelope that says "never a slope law" and a scale-trend
+    instrument that failed. "slope" is therefore now emitted ONLY when ALL
+    THREE per-record caps allow it (min-rule, fail closed on any absent field
+    — explicitly NOT a blunt ">=4 rungs" rule, which would rewrite G-12 /
+    08-stats-and-extrapolation.md section 2.1: those license a scale-trend fit
+    at >=3 COMPARABLE rungs, and that stays licensable here):
+
+      (i)   design.scale_language_max == "slope" — the machine-readable frozen
+            PER-RECORD ceiling (the prose ceiling lives in
+            extrapolation_envelope_verbatim; this field is its machine-readable
+            form, set at freeze time). Absent field => no ceiling permits
+            slope => fail closed to "sign-only". A declared ceiling below the
+            count license also caps it (e.g. a declared "none" caps a 2-rung
+            "sign-only").
+      (ii)  >=3 measured rungs lie inside design.model_scale_rungs — the
+            frozen list of COMPARABLE MODEL-SCALE rung labels (08-stats
+            section 2.1 precondition; a baseline label like a5-llm's R0 must
+            never inflate the count).
+      (iii) every JSON pointer in design.scale_trend_valid_metrics resolves to
+            boolean true in the analysis output — the registered section-2
+            trend machinery actually RAN and produced a VALID result (for the
+            a5-llm shape: /gates/separation_valid plus the trend Holm member;
+            a trend member that left the family instrument-invalid can never
+            license a slope).
+
+    The 1-rung "none" and 2-rung "sign-only" paths are byte-identical to the
+    pre-fix generator, so every issued license-"none"/"sign-only" verdict
+    (f2b-replicate et al.) regenerates unchanged.
+    """
+    license_ = "none" if len(rungs) < 2 else ("sign-only" if len(rungs) == 2 else "slope")
+    ceiling = design.get("scale_language_max")
+    if license_ == "slope":
+        comparable = [r for r in rungs if r in (design.get("model_scale_rungs") or [])]
+        trend_ptrs = design.get("scale_trend_valid_metrics") or []
+        trend_valid = (bool(trend_ptrs) and analysis is not None
+                       and all(kc.resolve_pointer(analysis, p) is True for p in trend_ptrs))
+        if not (ceiling == "slope" and len(comparable) >= 3 and trend_valid):
+            license_ = "sign-only"
+    if ceiling in SCALE_LICENSE_ORDER and SCALE_LICENSE_ORDER[ceiling] < SCALE_LICENSE_ORDER[license_]:
+        license_ = ceiling
+    return license_
+
+
 def select_eligible(records, frozen_sha, design):
     """P2 §3.1 step 3. Returns (eligible, excluded=[{seq, reason}])."""
     superseded = {r.get("target_seq") for r in records if r.get("event") == "supersede"}

@@ -557,7 +617,9 @@ def run(root, exp_id, agent_id, computed_at):
     rungs_reused = sorted({str(r["config"]["rung"]) for r in reused_rows
                            if isinstance(r.get("config"), dict) and "rung" in r["config"]})
     rungs = sorted(set(rungs_fresh) | set(rungs_reused))
-    license_ = "none" if len(rungs) < 2 else ("sign-only" if len(rungs) == 2 else "slope")
+    # Per-record min-rule licensing (a5-llm REFUTE remediation) — see
+    # scale_language_license's docstring for the three caps and their sources.
+    license_ = scale_language_license(effective["design"], rungs, analysis)

     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""verdict-gen — the verdict as a pure function of (frozen record, chained log).
     3	
     4	    python3 tools/registry/verdict-gen.py --experiment <id> --agent-id coordinator-1
     5	        [--root <repo>] [--computed-at 2026-07-12T18:00:00Z]
     6	
     7	Implements P2 §3.1 (minimal spine — the F1-critical path):
     8	  1. Load registry/experiments/<id>.json; recompute the frozen hash; require
     9	     equality with registry/frozen-index.json (ERR_P2_FROZEN_DRIFT — no verdict
    10	     of any kind is producible from a drifted record).
    11	  2. Amendment overlay (P2 §1.4 / §3.1 step 2): apply kot-amend/1 records from
    12	     registry/amendments/<id>/ in seq order as an overlay on the frozen record
    13	     BEFORE readout. kind "ops" / "pre-authorized-fallback" amendments may ONLY
    14	     fill a PINNED-AT-INPUTS placeholder or add a new runtime pin under /pins/;
    15	     a patch touching design scope (endpoints, thresholds, verdict_rules, kill
    16	     text, baselines, scale rungs, the pinned analysis script) aborts with
    17	     ERR_P2_DESIGN_AMEND. kind "design" amendments are REFUSED once the RT-5
    18	     cutoff has passed — the experiment is GNG-0-signed or its log contains a
    19	     phase:"final" run record — with ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT (the only lawful path
    20	     is a new experiment id with `supersedes`). An invalid amendment always
    21	     aborts; it is never silently skipped. The frozen record file and its
    22	     frozen_sha256 anchor are untouched; the deterministic overlay result is
    23	     logged in the verdict object as inputs.amended_record_sha256 alongside
    24	     inputs.amendments_applied, so the amended record is independently
    25	     re-derivable and verifiable from (frozen record, amendment files).
    26	  3. Select eligible run records from results-log/<id>.jsonl (chain-verified):
    27	     event=="run", phase=="final", exit=="ok", not superseded,
    28	     prereg_hash == frozen_sha256, config.seed in design.seeds (when seeds are
    29	     registered), config values within declared IV level sets. Exclusions are
    30	     listed with the failed test named — never silent.
    31	  3b. D9 reused-row eligibility (resource-optimization-plan.md §3 revision-1):
    32	     rows of ANOTHER record's log enter the analysis only via a frozen
    33	     kot-reg/2 reused_from block that re-verifies at consumption time
    34	     (RC-1..RC-8, maintainer-ratified ruling, filled pins, producer chain +
    35	     row hashes intact) AND an in-chain event:"reuse" witness restating the
    36	     exact row set. A reuse witness without a frozen declaration refuses
    37	     (ERR_P2_REUSE_UNDECLARED); >=1 fresh eligible run row is still required.
    38	  4. Completeness gate (minimal): an empty eligible set => INCOMPLETE-DATA.
    39	  5. Run the pinned analysis script (sha256 re-verified at execution;
    40	     ERR_P2_FROZEN_DRIFT on mismatch) with the eligible records as JSONL on
    41	     stdin; its stdout JSON is written to reports/auto/<id>/analysis-output.json.
    42	     ALL derived statistics live there and nowhere else (G-4).
    43	  6. Write the `unblind` log line (first time only) via log-append — the single
    44	     write path.
    45	  7. Evaluate the frozen verdict_rules top-down with the minimal expression
    46	     grammar (kot_common.eval_expr). A missing/null metric pointer => verdict
    47	     INCOMPLETE-DATA (fail closed — an analysis bug cannot default to a verdict).
    48	  8. A fired PASS is emitted as PASS-PENDING-AUDIT unless a CONFIRMED audit
    49	     record by a non-runner identity exists under registry/audits/<id>/ (G-6).
    50	  9. Emit registry/verdicts/<id>.json (canonical JSON; RT-14-linted).
    51	
    52	The rendered markdown report (P2 §3.3) is S7 report-gen's job — not built here.
    53	"""
    54	
    55	import argparse
    56	import datetime
    57	import glob
    58	import hashlib
    59	import json
    60	import os
    61	import re
    62	import subprocess
    63	import sys
    64	
    65	sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))
    66	import kot_common as kc
    67	
    68	# P2 §1.4 design scope — JSON-pointer prefixes that an "ops" /
    69	# "pre-authorized-fallback" amendment may NEVER touch (endpoints and
    70	# verdict_rules carry the thresholds; design carries baselines, scale rungs,
    71	# seeds, IV levels; the kill/envelope text and the pinned analysis script are
    72	# the frozen contract). Touching any of these is a design change and fails
    73	# closed with ERR_P2_DESIGN_AMEND.
    74	DESIGN_SCOPE_PREFIXES = (
    75	    "/endpoints",
    76	    "/verdict_rules",
    77	    "/design",
    78	    "/kill_criterion_verbatim",
    79	    "/extrapolation_envelope_verbatim",
    80	    "/pins/analysis_script",
    81	    "/hypotheses",
    82	    "/analysis_plan_ref",
    83	    "/prereg_doc",
    84	    "/coverage_requirement",
    85	    "/efficiency_relevant",
    86	    # D9: the reuse declaration is design scope — which rows a record consumes,
    87	    # under which RC basis, may never move post-freeze by ops amendment.
    88	    "/reused_from",
    89	    "/reuse_overrides",
    90	)
    91	
    92	# kot-reg schema selection (D9: kot-reg/2 records carry the reuse surface).
    93	SCHEMA_FILES = {"kot-reg/1": "kot-reg-1.json", "kot-reg/2": "kot-reg-2.json"}
    94	
    95	# Freeze bookkeeping — no amendment of ANY kind may touch these.
    96	FREEZE_FIELD_PREFIXES = ("/status", "/frozen_sha256", "/frozen_at", "/frozen_by", "/id", "/schema_version")
    97	
    98	# An ops pin value is a bare hex digest: 40 hex (git/HF revision sha) or
    99	# 64 hex (sha256: corpus digest, staged-bytes manifest). Nothing else.
   100	PIN_VALUE_RE = re.compile(r"^([0-9a-f]{40}|[0-9a-f]{64})$")
   101	
   102	
   103	def _touches(path, prefixes):
   104	    return any(path == p or path.startswith(p + "/") for p in prefixes)
   105	
   106	
   107	def fail(code, msg):
   108	    print("%s: %s" % (code, msg), file=sys.stderr)
   109	    sys.exit(1)
   110	
   111	
   112	def file_sha256(path):
   113	    h = hashlib.sha256()
   114	    with open(path, "rb") as f:
   115	        for chunk in iter(lambda: f.read(1 << 20), b""):
   116	            h.update(chunk)
   117	    return h.hexdigest()

   120	def gng0_signed(root, exp_id):
   121	    """True iff the experiment's frozen record is covered by the GNG-0 signoff."""
   122	    path = os.path.join(root, "registry", "gng0-signoff.json")
   123	    if not os.path.isfile(path):
   124	        return False
   125	    with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   126	        signoff = json.load(f)
   127	    return exp_id in signoff.get("frozen_records", {})
   128	
   129	
   130	def load_amendments(root, exp_id):
   131	    """Load, schema-validate, and RT-14-lint registry/amendments/<id>/*.json.
   132	
   133	    Returns [(relpath, amendment)] sorted by seq. Any malformed record aborts
   134	    (ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT) — an unreadable amendment is never skipped.
   135	    """
   136	    paths = sorted(glob.glob(os.path.join(root, "registry", "amendments", exp_id, "*.json")))
   137	    if not paths:
   138	        return []
   139	    schema_path = os.path.join(root, "registry", "schema", "kot-amend-1.json")
   140	    with open(schema_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   141	        schema = json.load(f)
   142	    out, seen = [], set()
   143	    for path in paths:
   144	        rel = os.path.relpath(path, root)
   145	        try:
   146	            with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   147	                am = json.load(f)
   148	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, UnicodeDecodeError) as e:
   149	            raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT", "%s: unparseable (%s)" % (rel, e))
   150	        errs = kc.validate_schema(am, schema)
   151	        if errs:
   152	            raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT", "%s: %s" % (rel, "; ".join(errs[:5])))
   153	        if am["experiment"] != exp_id:
   154	            raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   155	                              "%s: experiment %r != %r" % (rel, am["experiment"], exp_id))
   156	        prefix = os.path.basename(path).split("-", 1)[0]
   157	        if not prefix.isdigit() or int(prefix) != am["seq"]:
   158	            raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   159	                              "%s: filename seq prefix does not match seq=%r (<seq>-<slug>.json)"
   160	                              % (rel, am["seq"]))
   161	        if am["seq"] in seen:
   162	            raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT", "%s: duplicate amendment seq %d" % (rel, am["seq"]))
   163	        seen.add(am["seq"])
   164	        kc.check_identity_fields(am)
   165	        kc.require_no_account_strings(kc.canonical_bytes(am), rel)
   166	        out.append((rel, am))
   167	    out.sort(key=lambda t: t[1]["seq"])
   168	    return out
   169	
   170	
   171	def apply_amendment_overlay(root, exp_id, record, log_records):
   172	    """P2 §3.1 step 2: apply valid amendments in seq order as an overlay.
   173	
   174	    Returns (effective_record, applied_seqs, effective_sha256-or-None). The
   175	    frozen record file is never touched; the overlay is a pure function of
   176	    (frozen record, amendment files) so effective_sha256 is re-derivable by
   177	    any verifier. Fails closed on the first invalid amendment.
   178	    """
   179	    amendments = load_amendments(root, exp_id)
   180	    if not amendments:
   181	        return record, [], None
   182	
   183	    signed = gng0_signed(root, exp_id)
   184	    has_final = any(r.get("event") == "run" and r.get("phase") == "final" for r in log_records)
   185	    effective = record
   186	    applied = []
   187	    for rel, am in amendments:
   188	        for j, op in enumerate(am["patch"]):
   189	            if _touches(op["path"], FREEZE_FIELD_PREFIXES):
   190	                raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   191	                                  "%s patch[%d]: freeze bookkeeping field %s may never be amended"
   192	                                  % (rel, j, op["path"]))
   193	        if am["kind"] == "design":
   194	            # RT-5 cutoff: design amendments die at first raw-data exposure
   195	            # (first phase:"final" run record) — and a GNG-0-signed record's
   196	            # design is immutable outright. The only lawful path afterwards is
   197	            # a new experiment id with `supersedes` and a fresh freeze.
   198	            if signed or has_final:
   199	                raise kc.KotError(
   200	                    "ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   201	                    "%s: design amendment after the RT-5 cutoff (GNG-0-signed=%s, final-phase run "
   202	                    "present=%s) — the frozen design is immutable; supersede with a new experiment id"
   203	                    % (rel, signed, has_final))
   204	        else:  # "ops" | "pre-authorized-fallback"
   205	            if am["kind"] == "pre-authorized-fallback":
   206	                ptr = am.get("pre_authorized_by")
   207	                if not ptr:
   208	                    raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   209	                                      "%s: pre-authorized-fallback must name the frozen-record field "
   210	                                      "that pre-authorized it (pre_authorized_by)" % rel)
   211	                if kc.resolve_pointer(record, ptr) is kc._MISSING:
   212	                    raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   213	                                      "%s: pre_authorized_by pointer %r is not present in the frozen record"
   214	                                      % (rel, ptr))
   215	            for j, op in enumerate(am["patch"]):
   216	                where = "%s patch[%d] (%s %s)" % (rel, j, op["op"], op["path"])
   217	                if _touches(op["path"], DESIGN_SCOPE_PREFIXES):
   218	                    raise kc.KotError(
   219	                        "ERR_P2_DESIGN_AMEND",
   220	                        "%s: ops-scope amendment touches design scope (endpoints / thresholds / "
   221	                        "verdict_rules / kill text / baselines / scale rungs / pinned analysis) — "
   222	                        "refused; a design change needs a new experiment id" % where)
   223	                if not op["path"].startswith("/pins/"):
   224	                    raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   225	                                      "%s: an ops amendment may only fill a PINNED-AT-INPUTS placeholder "
   226	                                      "or add a runtime pin under /pins/" % where)
   227	                if op["op"] == "remove":
   228	                    raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   229	                                      "%s: ops amendments may not remove pins" % where)
   230	                value = op.get("value")
   231	                if not isinstance(value, str) or not PIN_VALUE_RE.match(value):
   232	                    raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   233	                                      "%s: pin value must be a bare 40- or 64-char lowercase hex digest, "
   234	                                      "got %r" % (where, value))
   235	                current = kc.resolve_pointer(effective, op["path"])
   236	                if op["op"] == "replace":
   237	                    if not (isinstance(current, str) and current.startswith(kc.PINNED_AT_INPUTS_PREFIX)):
   238	                        raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   239	                                          "%s: replace target is not a PINNED-AT-INPUTS placeholder "
   240	                                          "(current=%r) — frozen pins are immutable" % (where, current))
   241	                else:  # add
   242	                    if current is not kc._MISSING:
   243	                        raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   244	                                          "%s: add target already exists — an existing pin cannot be "
   245	                                          "overwritten" % where)
   246	        effective = kc.json_patch_apply(effective, am["patch"])
   247	        applied.append(am["seq"])
   248	
   249	    # Post-overlay integrity: the effective record must still validate against
   250	    # ITS OWN schema version (kot-reg/1 or /2) and be RT-14-clean — an overlay
   251	    # cannot smuggle in what a freeze would have refused.
   252	    sv = effective.get("schema_version")
   253	    if sv not in SCHEMA_FILES:
   254	        raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   255	                          "amended effective record schema_version %r is not one of %s"
   256	                          % (sv, sorted(SCHEMA_FILES)))
   257	    schema_path = os.path.join(root, "registry", "schema", SCHEMA_FILES[sv])
   258	    with open(schema_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   259	        reg_schema = json.load(f)
   260	    errs = kc.validate_schema(effective, reg_schema)
   261	    if errs:
   262	        raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   263	                          "amended effective record no longer validates against %s: %s"
   264	                          % (sv, "; ".join(errs[:5])))
   265	    kc.check_identity_fields(effective)
   266	    hashed = {k: v for k, v in effective.items() if k not in ("status", "frozen_sha256")}
   267	    kc.require_no_account_strings(kc.canonical_bytes(hashed), "amended effective record")
   268	    return effective, applied, kc.frozen_hash(effective)
   269	
   270	
   271	# Scale-language license total order for the min-rule (G-12 vocabulary is the
   272	# established one across all 17 issued verdicts: none / sign-only / slope).
   273	SCALE_LICENSE_ORDER = {"none": 0, "sign-only": 1, "slope": 2}
   274	
   275	
   276	def scale_language_license(design, rungs, analysis):
   277	    """Compute scale_language_licensed with per-record slope enforcement.
   278	
   279	    The G-12 count map (docs/research-plan/02-data-and-reporting.md:664) is
   280	    UNCHANGED: 1 rung -> "none", 2 -> "sign-only", >=3 -> "slope"-ELIGIBLE.
   281	    What changed (a5-llm Gate-A re-audit REFUTE 2026-07-11, adjudicated in
   282	    docs/next/a5-llm-refute-adjudication.md section 2.1/2.2, maintainer-approved
   283	    2026-07-11 issue #5; correction record
   284	    registry/corrections/a5-llm/2-scale-license-erratum.json): a raw distinct-
   285	    rung-LABEL count used to license "slope" gate-blind and envelope-blind —
   286	    for a5-llm it counted the R0 baseline label as a rung and emitted "slope"
   287	    against a frozen envelope that says "never a slope law" and a scale-trend
   288	    instrument that failed. "slope" is therefore now emitted ONLY when ALL
   289	    THREE per-record caps allow it (min-rule, fail closed on any absent field
   290	    — explicitly NOT a blunt ">=4 rungs" rule, which would rewrite G-12 /
   291	    08-stats-and-extrapolation.md section 2.1: those license a scale-trend fit
   292	    at >=3 COMPARABLE rungs, and that stays licensable here):
   293	
   294	      (i)   design.scale_language_max == "slope" — the machine-readable frozen
   295	            PER-RECORD ceiling (the prose ceiling lives in
   296	            extrapolation_envelope_verbatim; this field is its machine-readable
   297	            form, set at freeze time). Absent field => no ceiling permits
   298	            slope => fail closed to "sign-only". A declared ceiling below the
   299	            count license also caps it (e.g. a declared "none" caps a 2-rung
   300	            "sign-only").
   301	      (ii)  >=3 measured rungs lie inside design.model_scale_rungs — the
   302	            frozen list of COMPARABLE MODEL-SCALE rung labels (08-stats
   303	            section 2.1 precondition; a baseline label like a5-llm's R0 must
   304	            never inflate the count).
   305	      (iii) every JSON pointer in design.scale_trend_valid_metrics resolves to
   306	            boolean true in the analysis output — the registered section-2
   307	            trend machinery actually RAN and produced a VALID result (for the
   308	            a5-llm shape: /gates/separation_valid plus the trend Holm member;
   309	            a trend member that left the family instrument-invalid can never
   310	            license a slope).
   311	
   312	    The 1-rung "none" and 2-rung "sign-only" paths are byte-identical to the
   313	    pre-fix generator, so every issued license-"none"/"sign-only" verdict
   314	    (f2b-replicate et al.) regenerates unchanged.
   315	    """
   316	    license_ = "none" if len(rungs) < 2 else ("sign-only" if len(rungs) == 2 else "slope")
   317	    ceiling = design.get("scale_language_max")
   318	    if license_ == "slope":
   319	        comparable = [r for r in rungs if r in (design.get("model_scale_rungs") or [])]
   320	        trend_ptrs = design.get("scale_trend_valid_metrics") or []
   321	        trend_valid = (bool(trend_ptrs) and analysis is not None
   322	                       and all(kc.resolve_pointer(analysis, p) is True for p in trend_ptrs))
   323	        if not (ceiling == "slope" and len(comparable) >= 3 and trend_valid):
   324	            license_ = "sign-only"
   325	    if ceiling in SCALE_LICENSE_ORDER and SCALE_LICENSE_ORDER[ceiling] < SCALE_LICENSE_ORDER[license_]:
   326	        license_ = ceiling
   327	    return license_
   328	
   329	
   330	def select_eligible(records, frozen_sha, design):
   331	    """P2 §3.1 step 3. Returns (eligible, excluded=[{seq, reason}])."""
   332	    superseded = {r.get("target_seq") for r in records if r.get("event") == "supersede"}
   333	    iv_levels = {iv["name"]: iv["levels"] for iv in design.get("independent_vars", [])}
   334	    seeds = design.get("seeds", [])
   335	    eligible, excluded = [], []
   336	
   337	    def exclude(rec, reason):
   338	        excluded.append({"seq": rec["seq"], "reason": reason})
   339	
   340	    for rec in records:
   341	        if rec.get("event") != "run":
   342	            continue
   343	        if rec.get("phase") != "final":
   344	            exclude(rec, "phase!=final (%r)" % rec.get("phase"))
   345	            continue
   346	        if rec.get("exit") != "ok":
   347	            exclude(rec, "exit!=ok (%r)" % rec.get("exit"))
   348	            continue
   349	        if rec["seq"] in superseded:
   350	            exclude(rec, "superseded")
   351	            continue
   352	        if rec.get("prereg_hash") != frozen_sha:
   353	            exclude(rec, "prereg_hash mismatch")
   354	            continue
   355	        cfg = rec.get("config", {})
   356	        if seeds and "seed" in cfg and cfg["seed"] not in seeds:
   357	            exclude(rec, "seed %r not registered" % cfg["seed"])
   358	            continue
   359	        bad_level = None
   360	        for name, levels in iv_levels.items():
   361	            if name in cfg and cfg[name] not in levels:
   362	                bad_level = "config.%s=%r not in declared levels" % (name, cfg[name])
   363	                break
   364	        if bad_level:
   365	            exclude(rec, bad_level)
   366	            continue
   367	        eligible.append(rec)
   368	    return eligible, excluded

   371	def confirmed_audit(root, exp_id, runner_ids):
   372	    """Return the path of a CONFIRMED audit by a non-runner identity, or None."""
   373	    for path in sorted(glob.glob(os.path.join(root, "registry", "audits", exp_id, "*.json"))):
   374	        with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   375	            audit = json.load(f)
   376	        if audit.get("outcome") != "CONFIRMED":
   377	            continue
   378	        auditor = audit.get("auditor")
   379	        if auditor in runner_ids:
   380	            raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_SELF_AUDIT",
   381	                              "%s: auditor %r also appears as a runner in the eligible log" % (path, auditor))
   382	        return os.path.relpath(path, root)
   383	    return None

   386	def main():
   387	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Compute an experiment verdict purely from frozen record + log.")
   388	    ap.add_argument("--experiment", required=True)
   389	    ap.add_argument("--agent-id", required=True, help="pseudonym stamped on the unblind log line")
   390	    ap.add_argument("--root", default=None)
   391	    ap.add_argument("--computed-at", default=None, help="UTC timestamp override (byte-determinism/tests)")
   392	    args = ap.parse_args()
   393	
   394	    root = args.root or os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   395	    exp_id = args.experiment
   396	    try:
   397	        run(root, exp_id, args.agent_id, args.computed_at)
   398	    except kc.KotError as e:
   399	        fail(e.code, str(e).split(": ", 1)[1])
   400	    except FileNotFoundError as e:
   401	        fail("ERR_P2_IO", str(e))

   404	def run(root, exp_id, agent_id, computed_at):
   405	    kc.require_pseudonym(agent_id, "--agent-id")
   406	
   407	    # Step 1 — frozen record integrity.
   408	    rec_path = os.path.join(root, "registry", "experiments", "%s.json" % exp_id)
   409	    index_path = os.path.join(root, "registry", "frozen-index.json")
   410	    with open(rec_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   411	        record = json.load(f)
   412	    with open(index_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   413	        index = json.load(f)
   414	    if exp_id not in index:
   415	        raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_NOT_FROZEN", "%s absent from frozen-index.json" % exp_id)
   416	    frozen_sha = index[exp_id]
   417	    if kc.frozen_hash(record) != frozen_sha or record.get("frozen_sha256") != frozen_sha:
   418	        raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_FROZEN_DRIFT",
   419	                          "%s: frozen record bytes drifted from frozen-index — no verdict is producible" % exp_id)
   420	
   421	    # Step 2 — amendment overlay (fail closed, never silently skipped). The
   422	    # chain-verified log is read first: it is the RT-5 cutoff witness. The
   423	    # overlay never touches the frozen file; `record` stays the frozen anchor
   424	    # (prereg_hash eligibility is checked against frozen_sha), `effective` is
   425	    # what readout runs against.
   426	    log_path = os.path.join(root, "results-log", "%s.jsonl" % exp_id)
   427	    records, raw_lines = kc.read_log(log_path)
   428	    effective, amendments_applied, amended_sha = apply_amendment_overlay(root, exp_id, record, records)
   429	
   430	    # Step 3 — eligibility.
   431	    eligible, excluded = select_eligible(records, frozen_sha, effective["design"])
   432	    tail_sha = kc.log_tail_sha256(raw_lines)
   433	    runner_ids = {r["runner"] for r in eligible}
   434	
   435	    # Step 3b — D9 reused-row eligibility (resource-optimization-plan.md §3
   436	    # revision-1). Reused producer rows enter the analysis ONLY when ALL hold:
   437	    #   - the frozen (effective) record is kot-reg/2 with a reused_from block
   438	    #     (an in-log reuse witness without a frozen declaration is refused);
   439	    #   - the ruling is maintainer-ratified (kc.check_record_reuse enforces);
   440	    #   - every RC-1..RC-8 machine check re-verifies at consumption time,
   441	    #     including RC-2 identity with placeholders now FILLED (no
   442	    #     indeterminate pins may license consumption);
   443	    #   - an in-chain RC-6 witness (event:"reuse") restates the exact verified
   444	    #     row set {seqs, row_hashes} for each block.
   445	    # Reused rows NEVER substitute for fresh evidence: the completeness gate
   446	    # below still requires >=1 fresh eligible run row.
   447	    reuse_witnesses = [r for r in records if r.get("event") == "reuse"]
   448	    reused_rows, reused_summary = [], []
   449	    if reuse_witnesses and not (effective.get("reused_from") or []):
   450	        raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_REUSE_UNDECLARED",
   451	                          "log carries %d reuse witness line(s) but the frozen record declares no "
   452	                          "reused_from block" % len(reuse_witnesses))
   453	    if effective.get("reused_from") and eligible:
   454	        for res in kc.check_record_reuse(effective, root, mode="verdict"):
   455	            block, rows, seqs = res["block"], res["rows"], res["seqs"]
   456	            verified = {rec_p["seq"]: kc.sha256_hex(raw_p) for rec_p, raw_p in rows}
   457	            witness_ok = False
   458	            for w in reuse_witnesses:
   459	                wr = w.get("reuse") or {}
   460	                if (wr.get("producer") == block["producer"]
   461	                        and wr.get("producer_frozen_sha256") == block["producer_frozen_sha256"]
   462	                        and dict(zip(wr.get("seqs", []), wr.get("row_hashes", []))) == verified):
   463	                    witness_ok = True
   464	                    break
   465	            if not witness_ok:
   466	                raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_REUSE_NO_WITNESS",
   467	                                  "no event:\"reuse\" line in the consumer log restates the verified "
   468	                                  "row set for producer %s (RC-6 in-chain witness; append it via "
   469	                                  "log-append before the verdict)" % block["producer"])
   470	            for rec_p, raw_p in rows:
   471	                aug = dict(rec_p)
   472	                aug["reuse_provenance"] = {
   473	                    "producer": block["producer"],
   474	                    "producer_frozen_sha256": block["producer_frozen_sha256"],
   475	                    "row_sha256": verified[rec_p["seq"]],
   476	                    "role": block["role"],
   477	                }
   478	                reused_rows.append(aug)
   479	            reused_summary.append({
   480	                "producer": block["producer"],
   481	                "producer_frozen_sha256": block["producer_frozen_sha256"],
   482	                "role": block["role"],
   483	                "seqs": seqs,
   484	                "rows_sha256": kc.canonical_sha256([verified[s] for s in seqs]),
   485	            })
   486	
   487	    verdict = None
   488	    fired_index = None
   489	    fired_rule = None
   490	    analysis = None
   491	    analysis_output_sha = None
   492	    # Ops amendments cannot touch /pins/analysis_script, so this pin is
   493	    # byte-identical between `record` and `effective`; read it from the
   494	    # effective record for uniformity.
   495	    script_sha = effective["pins"]["analysis_script"]["sha256"]
   496	    endpoint_results = []
   497	    coverage = None
   498	
   499	    if not eligible:
   500	        # Step 4 — completeness gate (minimal form).
   501	        verdict = "INCOMPLETE-DATA"
   502	    else:
   503	        # Step 5 — pinned analysis script over eligible records only.
   504	        script_path = os.path.join(root, effective["pins"]["analysis_script"]["path"])
   505	        got = file_sha256(script_path)
   506	        if got != script_sha:
   507	            raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_FROZEN_DRIFT",
   508	                              "pinned analysis script %s sha256 %s != frozen pin %s"
   509	                              % (effective["pins"]["analysis_script"]["path"], got, script_sha))
   510	        # Reused rows (verified above) follow the fresh eligible rows on stdin,
   511	        # each carrying reuse_provenance so the pinned analysis can distinguish
   512	        # fresh from reused (and apply the frozen selection-adjusted inference
   513	        # where RC-8 requires it).
   514	        stdin_payload = "".join(kc.canonical_dumps(r) + "\n" for r in eligible + reused_rows)
   515	        proc = subprocess.run(
   516	            ["nice", "-n", "10", sys.executable, script_path],
   517	            input=stdin_payload.encode("utf-8"), capture_output=True, cwd=root,
   518	        )
   519	        if proc.returncode != 0:
   520	            raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_ANALYSIS",
   521	                              "pinned analysis script exited %d: %s"
   522	                              % (proc.returncode, proc.stderr.decode("utf-8", "replace")[-2000:]))
   523	        try:
   524	            analysis = json.loads(proc.stdout.decode("utf-8"))
   525	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, UnicodeDecodeError) as e:
   526	            raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_ANALYSIS", "analysis output is not JSON: %s" % e)
   527	        out_dir = os.path.join(root, "reports", "auto", exp_id)
   528	        os.makedirs(out_dir, exist_ok=True)
   529	        out_path = os.path.join(out_dir, "analysis-output.json")
   530	        kc.write_canonical_json(out_path, analysis)
   531	        analysis_output_sha = file_sha256(out_path)
   532	
   533	        # Step 6 — unblind line, first time only, via the single write path.
   534	        if not any(r.get("event") == "unblind" for r in records):
   535	            unblind = {"event": "unblind", "prereg_hash": frozen_sha}
   536	            p = subprocess.run(
   537	                [sys.executable, os.path.join(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)), "log-append.py"),
   538	                 "--experiment", exp_id, "--agent-id", agent_id, "--record", "-", "--root", root],
   539	                input=kc.canonical_dumps(unblind).encode("utf-8"), capture_output=True,
   540	            )
   541	            if p.returncode != 0:
   542	                raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_CHAIN", "could not write unblind line: %s"
   543	                                  % p.stderr.decode("utf-8", "replace"))
   544	            records, raw_lines = kc.read_log(log_path)
   545	            tail_sha = kc.log_tail_sha256(raw_lines)
   546	
   547	        # G-7 (scoped): coverage is required only when the frozen record declares it.
   548	        # coverage_requirement.source names WHERE the coverage block comes from
   549	        # (P2 §2 G-7: "absent from eligible runs' metrics or the verdict object"):
   550	        #   - "self" / this experiment's id: the experiment measures its own
   551	        #     coverage and every eligible run carries metrics.coverage (m0b).
   552	        #   - any other experiment id: the published coverage from that
   553	        #     experiment's verdict object is RESTATED here (P3 m0b.close:
   554	        #     "publish coverage + rung; restated in every later verdict").
   555	        #     Fail closed unless that verdict exists, carries a coverage
   556	        #     block, and is itself audit-CONFIRMED (G-6 — an unaudited
   557	        #     coverage number must not silently license downstream verdicts).
   558	        if "coverage_requirement" in effective:
   559	            cov_source = effective["coverage_requirement"].get("source", "self")
   560	            if cov_source in ("self", exp_id):
   561	                coverage = eligible[-1].get("metrics", {}).get("coverage")
   562	                if not coverage:
   563	                    raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_NO_COVERAGE",
   564	                                      "coverage_requirement declared but eligible runs carry no metrics.coverage block")
   565	            else:
   566	                src_path = os.path.join(root, "registry", "verdicts", "%s.json" % cov_source)
   567	                if not os.path.exists(src_path):
   568	                    raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_NO_COVERAGE",
   569	                                      "coverage_requirement.source=%r but registry/verdicts/%s.json does not exist"
   570	                                      % (cov_source, cov_source))
   571	                with open(src_path, encoding="utf-8") as f:
   572	                    src_verdict = json.load(f)
   573	                coverage = src_verdict.get("coverage")
   574	                if not coverage:
   575	                    raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_NO_COVERAGE",
   576	                                      "coverage_requirement.source=%r but that verdict object carries no coverage block"
   577	                                      % cov_source)
   578	                if src_verdict.get("audit", {}).get("state") != "CONFIRMED":
   579	                    raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_NO_COVERAGE",
   580	                                      "coverage_requirement.source=%r but that verdict's audit state is %r, not CONFIRMED"
   581	                                      % (cov_source, src_verdict.get("audit", {}).get("state")))
   582	
   583	        for ep in effective["endpoints"]:
   584	            v = kc.resolve_pointer(analysis, ep["metric"])
   585	            endpoint_results.append({
   586	                "id": ep["id"], "role": ep["role"], "metric": ep["metric"],
   587	                "value": None if v is kc._MISSING else v,
   588	            })
   589	
   590	        # Step 7 — frozen verdict rules, first match wins; fail closed on gaps.
   591	        try:
   592	            for i, rule in enumerate(effective["verdict_rules"]):
   593	                if kc.eval_expr(rule["when"], analysis):
   594	                    verdict = rule["verdict"]
   595	                    fired_index = i
   596	                    fired_rule = rule["when"]
   597	                    break
   598	        except kc.MissingMetric as e:
   599	            verdict = "INCOMPLETE-DATA"
   600	            fired_rule = {"missing_metric": e.pointer}
   601	        if verdict is None:
   602	            # unreachable when the freeze-time catch-all lint held; fail closed anyway
   603	            verdict = "INCOMPLETE-DATA"
   604	
   605	    # Step 8 — audit gate on PASS.
   606	    audit_state = {"state": "N/A", "path": None}
   607	    if verdict == "PASS":
   608	        audit_path = confirmed_audit(root, exp_id, runner_ids)
   609	        if audit_path is None:
   610	            verdict = "PASS-PENDING-AUDIT"
   611	            audit_state = {"state": "PENDING", "path": None}
   612	        else:
   613	            audit_state = {"state": "CONFIRMED", "path": audit_path}
   614	
   615	    rungs_fresh = sorted({str(r["config"]["rung"]) for r in eligible
   616	                          if isinstance(r.get("config"), dict) and "rung" in r["config"]})
   617	    rungs_reused = sorted({str(r["config"]["rung"]) for r in reused_rows
   618	                           if isinstance(r.get("config"), dict) and "rung" in r["config"]})
   619	    rungs = sorted(set(rungs_fresh) | set(rungs_reused))
   620	    # Per-record min-rule licensing (a5-llm REFUTE remediation) — see
   621	    # scale_language_license's docstring for the three caps and their sources.
   622	    license_ = scale_language_license(effective["design"], rungs, analysis)
   623	
   624	    verdict_obj = {
   625	        "schema_version": "kot-verdict/1",
   626	        "experiment": exp_id,
   627	        "hypotheses": effective["hypotheses"],
   628	        "verdict": verdict,
   629	        "fired_rule_index": fired_index,
   630	        "fired_rule": fired_rule,
   631	        "computed_at": computed_at or datetime.datetime.utcnow().strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),
   632	        "inputs": {
   633	            "frozen_sha256": frozen_sha,
   634	            "amendments_applied": amendments_applied,
   635	            "amended_record_sha256": amended_sha,
   636	            "log_tail_sha256": tail_sha,
   637	            "eligible_runs": len(eligible),
   638	            "excluded_runs": excluded,
   639	            "analysis_output_sha256": analysis_output_sha,
   640	            "analysis_script_sha256": script_sha,
   641	        },
   642	        "endpoint_results": endpoint_results,
   643	        "coverage": coverage,
   644	        "rungs_measured": rungs,
   645	        "scale_language_licensed": license_,
   646	        # (fields below added only for reuse-consuming records, so pre-D9
   647	        # verdict objects keep their exact shape)
   648	        "kill_criterion_verbatim": effective["kill_criterion_verbatim"],
   649	        "extrapolation_envelope_verbatim": effective["extrapolation_envelope_verbatim"],
   650	        "audit": audit_state,
   651	    }
   652	    if reused_summary:
   653	        # Transparency for the auditor (RC-6): what was consumed, from where,
   654	        # and which rungs the record measured fresh vs inherited via reuse —
   655	        # any scale-language license that leans on reused rungs is visible.
   656	        verdict_obj["inputs"]["reused"] = reused_summary
   657	        verdict_obj["rungs_fresh"] = rungs_fresh
   658	        verdict_obj["rungs_reused_only"] = sorted(set(rungs_reused) - set(rungs_fresh))
   659	
   660	    # RT-14 applies to verdict objects too (they are re-hashed by audits).
   661	    kc.check_identity_fields(verdict_obj)
   662	    kc.require_no_account_strings(kc.canonical_bytes(verdict_obj), "verdict object")
   663	
   664	    verdict_dir = os.path.join(root, "registry", "verdicts")
   665	    os.makedirs(verdict_dir, exist_ok=True)
   666	    kc.write_canonical_json(os.path.join(verdict_dir, "%s.json" % exp_id), verdict_obj)
   667	    print(json.dumps(verdict_obj, indent=2, sort_keys=True))

item.text: A material completeness concern is already visible: the generator requires three new frozen design fields, while the test file itself says the registry schemas do not admit them and bypasses the normal freeze path by hand. I’m checking whether that makes the preservation case operationally impossible, and whether missing JSON pointers fail closed or instead crash generation.

docs/next/a5-llm-refute-adjudication.md:90:1. **Frozen per-record ceiling, machine-readable.** The frozen `extrapolation_envelope_verbatim` is the binding ceiling, but it is prose; add a design field (e.g. `design.scale_language_max: "sign-only"`) set at freeze time and validated by `prereg-freeze` against the envelope text. For a5-llm the envelope says "never a slope law" ⇒ ceiling `sign-only`.
docs/next/a5-llm-refute-adjudication.md:91:2. **G-12 count over COMPARABLE MODEL-SCALE rungs only.** Add `design.model_scale_rungs` identifying the ladder rungs (for a5-llm {R1,R2,R3}; R0's engine/abstain-all/answer-all baseline cells are not model-scale rungs and must not inflate the count). The G-12 map itself is UNCHANGED: 1→`none`, 2→`sign-only`, ≥3→`slope`-eligible. Maintainer note: reading G-12's `rungs_measured` as "comparable model-scale rungs" (which §2.1's "comparable rungs" precondition already implies) is a small normative clarification to ratify alongside the code change (decision #1a).

     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3

     6	Pins the per-record min-rule fix adjudicated in

     8	2026-07-11, issue #5) and documented in
     9	registry/corrections/a5-llm/2-scale-license-erratum.json:
    10	
    11	  REGRESSION    — the a5-llm configuration (4 rung labels incl. the R0
    12	                  baseline, envelope ceiling sign-only, failed trend gates)
    13	                  now licenses "sign-only", never "slope"; verified BOTH
    14	                  directly against the real frozen a5-llm record + its real
    15	                  analysis output AND end-to-end through the verdict-gen CLI
    16	                  on a fixture mirroring that configuration. (The real
    17	                  registry/verdicts/a5-llm.json is NOT regenerated here —
    18	                  S7 regeneration is coordinator-gated, adjudication
    19	                  decision #1b/#6 — so the regression is proven on the same
    20	                  pure function + the frozen inputs, without mutating any
    21	                  issued artifact.)
    22	  PRESERVATION  — G-12 (02-data-and-reporting.md:664) and 08-stats section
    23	                  2.1 are UNCHANGED: >=3 COMPARABLE model-scale rungs with a
    24	                  valid trend result and a slope ceiling STILL license
    25	                  "slope" — the fix is not a blunt ">=4 rungs" rule.
    26	  CEILING       — a frozen per-record ceiling of sign-only refuses "slope"
    27	                  even with many comparable rungs and a valid trend result.
    28	
    29	NOTE (conservative reading, flagged in the remediation result): the new
    30	machine-readable design fields (scale_language_max, model_scale_rungs,
    31	scale_trend_valid_metrics) are not yet admitted by the kot-reg schemas
    32	(design.additionalProperties=false), so fixtures carrying them are
    33	hand-frozen (frozen_hash + frozen-index written directly, exactly what
    34	verdict-gen verifies). Admitting the fields at prereg-freeze time — with the

    36	adjudication prescribes — is a named follow-up for the coordinator.

    77	class TestRealRecordRegression(unittest.TestCase):
    78	    """The a5-llm / f2 regressions against the REAL frozen artifacts."""
    79	
    80	    def setUp(self):
    81	        self.index = load_json("registry/frozen-index.json")
    82	
    83	    def test_a5llm_licenses_sign_only_not_slope(self):
    84	        # REGRESSION: the exact frozen a5-llm inputs (design, measured rungs
    85	        # incl. R0, real analysis output with failed trend gates) now yield
    86	        # sign-only — matching the frozen envelope ("never a slope law") and
    87	        # correction/2's binding reading.
    88	        record = load_json("registry/experiments/a5-llm.json")
    89	        verdict = load_json("registry/verdicts/a5-llm.json")
    90	        analysis = load_json("reports/auto/a5-llm/analysis-output.json")
    91	        self.assertEqual(verdict["rungs_measured"], ["R0", "R1", "R2", "R3"])
    92	        self.assertEqual(verdict["scale_language_licensed"], "slope",
    93	                         "the issued (defective) verdict object must stay byte-identical")
    94	        got = vg.scale_language_license(record["design"], verdict["rungs_measured"], analysis)
    95	        self.assertEqual(got, "sign-only")
    96	
    97	    def test_a5llm_failed_trend_instrument_blocks_slope_regardless_of_ceiling(self):
    98	        # Even if a5-llm HAD declared a slope ceiling and its model-scale
    99	        # rungs, the failed trend instrument (separation_valid=false,
   100	        # scale_trend_rag=false) alone forces sign-only (cap iii).
   101	        record = load_json("registry/experiments/a5-llm.json")
   102	        analysis = load_json("reports/auto/a5-llm/analysis-output.json")
   103	        self.assertIs(analysis["gates"]["separation_valid"], False)
   104	        self.assertIs(analysis["analysis"]["holm"]["scale_trend_rag"], False)
   105	        design = dict(record["design"])
   106	        design["scale_language_max"] = "slope"
   107	        design["model_scale_rungs"] = ["R1", "R2", "R3"]
   108	        design["scale_trend_valid_metrics"] = ["/gates/separation_valid",
   109	                                               "/analysis/holm/scale_trend_rag"]
   110	        got = vg.scale_language_license(design, ["R0", "R1", "R2", "R3"], analysis)
   111	        self.assertEqual(got, "sign-only")

   114	        # f2 hygiene regression (correction registry/corrections/f2/2-*):

   116	        # valid trend fit in the analysis output => sign-only.

   150	class TestScaleLicenseUnit(unittest.TestCase):
   151	    """Pure-function truth table for the three caps."""
   152	
   153	    DESIGN_FULL = {
   154	        "scale_language_max": "slope",
   155	        "model_scale_rungs": ["R1", "R2", "R3", "R4"],
   156	        "scale_trend_valid_metrics": ["/gates/separation_valid", "/gates/scale_trend"],
   157	    }
   158	    ANALYSIS_OK = {"gates": {"separation_valid": True, "scale_trend": True}}
   159	    ANALYSIS_BAD = {"gates": {"separation_valid": False, "scale_trend": True}}
   160	
   161	    def lic(self, design, rungs, analysis=None):
   162	        return vg.scale_language_license(design, rungs, analysis)
   163	
   164	    def test_g12_low_counts_unchanged(self):
   165	        # Byte-identical legacy behaviour below 3 rungs (f2b-replicate et al.).
   166	        self.assertEqual(self.lic({}, []), "none")
   167	        self.assertEqual(self.lic({}, ["R1"]), "none")
   168	        self.assertEqual(self.lic({}, ["R1", "R3"]), "sign-only")
   169	        self.assertEqual(self.lic(self.DESIGN_FULL, ["R1", "R3"], self.ANALYSIS_OK), "sign-only")
   170	
   171	    def test_preservation_three_comparable_rungs_license_slope(self):
   172	        # PRESERVATION (G-12 / 08-stats 2.1): exactly 3 comparable rungs +
   173	        # slope ceiling + valid trend => slope. NOT a blunt >=4 rule.
   174	        d = dict(self.DESIGN_FULL, model_scale_rungs=["R1", "R2", "R3"])
   175	        self.assertEqual(self.lic(d, ["R1", "R2", "R3"], self.ANALYSIS_OK), "slope")
   176	
   177	    def test_baseline_rung_label_never_inflates_the_count(self):
   178	        # The a5-llm R0 defect: 3 measured labels, only 2 comparable => no slope.
   179	        d = dict(self.DESIGN_FULL, model_scale_rungs=["R1", "R2", "R3"])
   180	        self.assertEqual(self.lic(d, ["R0", "R1", "R2"], self.ANALYSIS_OK), "sign-only")
   181	        # 4 labels incl. R0, 3 comparable => slope still licensable when valid.
   182	        self.assertEqual(self.lic(d, ["R0", "R1", "R2", "R3"], self.ANALYSIS_OK), "slope")
   183	
   184	    def test_ceiling_caps_slope(self):
   185	        # CEILING: sign-only ceiling refuses slope even with many rungs.
   186	        d = dict(self.DESIGN_FULL, scale_language_max="sign-only")
   187	        self.assertEqual(self.lic(d, ["R1", "R2", "R3", "R4"], self.ANALYSIS_OK), "sign-only")
   188	        # A declared "none" ceiling caps even a 2-rung sign.
   189	        d = dict(self.DESIGN_FULL, scale_language_max="none")
   190	        self.assertEqual(self.lic(d, ["R1", "R3"], self.ANALYSIS_OK), "none")
   191	
   192	    def test_absent_ceiling_fails_closed(self):
   193	        d = {k: v for k, v in self.DESIGN_FULL.items() if k != "scale_language_max"}
   194	        self.assertEqual(self.lic(d, ["R1", "R2", "R3", "R4"], self.ANALYSIS_OK), "sign-only")
   195	
   196	    def test_invalid_trend_result_fails_closed(self):
   197	        self.assertEqual(self.lic(self.DESIGN_FULL, ["R1", "R2", "R3"], self.ANALYSIS_BAD),
   198	                         "sign-only")
   199	        # Declared pointer missing from the analysis output => fail closed.
   200	        d = dict(self.DESIGN_FULL, scale_trend_valid_metrics=["/gates/nonexistent"])
   201	        self.assertEqual(self.lic(d, ["R1", "R2", "R3"], self.ANALYSIS_OK), "sign-only")
   202	        # No declared trend metrics => fail closed.
   203	        d = {k: v for k, v in self.DESIGN_FULL.items() if k != "scale_trend_valid_metrics"}
   204	        self.assertEqual(self.lic(d, ["R1", "R2", "R3"], self.ANALYSIS_OK), "sign-only")
   205	        # No analysis at all (INCOMPLETE-DATA path) => fail closed.
   206	        self.assertEqual(self.lic(self.DESIGN_FULL, ["R1", "R2", "R3"], None), "sign-only")
   207	
   208	    def test_legacy_record_without_fields_never_slopes(self):
   209	        # Pre-fix records (a5-llm/f2 as frozen) carry none of the three
   210	        # fields: slope is unreachable, sign-only is the max.
   211	        self.assertEqual(self.lic({}, ["R0", "R1", "R2", "R3"], self.ANALYSIS_OK), "sign-only")

   214	class TestVerdictGenEndToEnd(SpineFixture):
   215	    """The fixed generator through the real CLI on hand-frozen fixtures."""
   216	
   217	    def hand_freeze(self, rec):
   218	        # Direct freeze (frozen_hash + index), bypassing prereg-freeze: the
   219	        # kot-reg schemas do not yet admit the three new design fields (see
   220	        # module docstring). verdict-gen verifies exactly these two anchors.
   221	        rec = dict(rec)
   222	        rec["status"] = "FROZEN"
   223	        rec["frozen_at"] = "2026-07-08T00:00:00Z"
   224	        rec["frozen_by"] = "coordinator-1"
   225	        rec.pop("frozen_sha256", None)
   226	        sha = kc.frozen_hash(rec)
   227	        rec["frozen_sha256"] = sha
   228	        kc.write_canonical_json(self.path("registry/experiments/%s.json" % rec["id"]), rec)
   229	        idx_path = self.path("registry/frozen-index.json")
   230	        idx = {}
   231	        if os.path.exists(idx_path):
   232	            with open(idx_path, encoding="utf-8") as f:
   233	                idx = json.load(f)
   234	        idx[rec["id"]] = sha
   235	        kc.write_canonical_json(idx_path, idx)
   236	        return sha

   238	    def rung_record(self, exp_id, rung_levels, design_extra=None, trend_ok=True):
   239	        script = "analysis/mock_trend_%s.py" % ("ok" if trend_ok else "fail")
   240	        self.write(script, MOCK_ANALYSIS_TREND_OK if trend_ok else MOCK_ANALYSIS_TREND_FAIL)
   241	        rec = self.base_record(exp_id=exp_id)
   242	        rec["design"]["independent_vars"].append({"name": "rung", "levels": rung_levels})
   243	        rec["design"]["scale_rungs"] = rung_levels
   244	        rec["design"]["seeds"] = list(range(len(rung_levels)))
   245	        rec["design"].update(design_extra or {})
   246	        rec["pins"]["analysis_script"] = {
   247	            "path": script, "sha256": sha256_file(self.path(script)),
   248	            "output_fields": ["/ratio", "/n_runs", "/gates/separation_valid",
   249	                              "/gates/scale_trend"],
   250	        }
   251	        return rec

   257	            "config": {"arm": "kotk2-vs-gloss", "seed": seed, "rung": rung},
   258	            "metrics": {"kernel_pack_bytes": 300, "gloss_zstd_bytes": 990, "n_records": 10},
   259	            "exit": "ok",
   260	        }
   261	        p = run_cli(LOG_APPEND, "--experiment", exp_id, "--agent-id", "runner-1",
   262	                    "--record", "-", "--root", self.root,
   263	                    "--ts", "2026-07-08T00:00:0%dZ" % seed, stdin=json.dumps(body))
   264	        self.assertEqual(p.returncode, 0, p.stderr)
   265	        return p

   271	# Scale-language license total order for the min-rule (G-12 vocabulary is the
   272	# established one across all 17 issued verdicts: none / sign-only / slope).
   273	SCALE_LICENSE_ORDER = {"none": 0, "sign-only": 1, "slope": 2}
   274	
   275	
   276	def scale_language_license(design, rungs, analysis):
   277	    """Compute scale_language_licensed with per-record slope enforcement.
   278	
   279	    The G-12 count map (docs/research-plan/02-data-and-reporting.md:664) is
   280	    UNCHANGED: 1 rung -> "none", 2 -> "sign-only", >=3 -> "slope"-ELIGIBLE.
   281	    What changed (a5-llm Gate-A re-audit REFUTE 2026-07-11, adjudicated in
   282	    docs/next/a5-llm-refute-adjudication.md section 2.1/2.2, maintainer-approved
   283	    2026-07-11 issue #5; correction record
   284	    registry/corrections/a5-llm/2-scale-license-erratum.json): a raw distinct-
   285	    rung-LABEL count used to license "slope" gate-blind and envelope-blind —
   286	    for a5-llm it counted the R0 baseline label as a rung and emitted "slope"
   287	    against a frozen envelope that says "never a slope law" and a scale-trend
   288	    instrument that failed. "slope" is therefore now emitted ONLY when ALL
   289	    THREE per-record caps allow it (min-rule, fail closed on any absent field
   290	    — explicitly NOT a blunt ">=4 rungs" rule, which would rewrite G-12 /
   291	    08-stats-and-extrapolation.md section 2.1: those license a scale-trend fit
   292	    at >=3 COMPARABLE rungs, and that stays licensable here):
   293	
   294	      (i)   design.scale_language_max == "slope" — the machine-readable frozen
   295	            PER-RECORD ceiling (the prose ceiling lives in
   296	            extrapolation_envelope_verbatim; this field is its machine-readable
   297	            form, set at freeze time). Absent field => no ceiling permits
   298	            slope => fail closed to "sign-only". A declared ceiling below the
   299	            count license also caps it (e.g. a declared "none" caps a 2-rung
   300	            "sign-only").
   301	      (ii)  >=3 measured rungs lie inside design.model_scale_rungs — the
   302	            frozen list of COMPARABLE MODEL-SCALE rung labels (08-stats
   303	            section 2.1 precondition; a baseline label like a5-llm's R0 must
   304	            never inflate the count).
   305	      (iii) every JSON pointer in design.scale_trend_valid_metrics resolves to
   306	            boolean true in the analysis output — the registered section-2
   307	            trend machinery actually RAN and produced a VALID result (for the
   308	            a5-llm shape: /gates/separation_valid plus the trend Holm member;
   309	            a trend member that left the family instrument-invalid can never
   310	            license a slope).
   311	
   312	    The 1-rung "none" and 2-rung "sign-only" paths are byte-identical to the
   313	    pre-fix generator, so every issued license-"none"/"sign-only" verdict
   314	    (f2b-replicate et al.) regenerates unchanged.
   315	    """
   316	    license_ = "none" if len(rungs) < 2 else ("sign-only" if len(rungs) == 2 else "slope")
   317	    ceiling = design.get("scale_language_max")
   318	    if license_ == "slope":
   319	        comparable = [r for r in rungs if r in (design.get("model_scale_rungs") or [])]
   320	        trend_ptrs = design.get("scale_trend_valid_metrics") or []
   321	        trend_valid = (bool(trend_ptrs) and analysis is not None
   322	                       and all(kc.resolve_pointer(analysis, p) is True for p in trend_ptrs))
   323	        if not (ceiling == "slope" and len(comparable) >= 3 and trend_valid):
   324	            license_ = "sign-only"
   325	    if ceiling in SCALE_LICENSE_ORDER and SCALE_LICENSE_ORDER[ceiling] < SCALE_LICENSE_ORDER[license_]:
   326	        license_ = ceiling
   327	    return license_
   328

   330	def select_eligible(records, frozen_sha, design):
   331	    """P2 §3.1 step 3. Returns (eligible, excluded=[{seq, reason}])."""
   332	    superseded = {r.get("target_seq") for r in records if r.get("event") == "supersede"}
   333	    iv_levels = {iv["name"]: iv["levels"] for iv in design.get("independent_vars", [])}
   334	    seeds = design.get("seeds", [])
   335	    eligible, excluded = [], []
   336	
   337	    def exclude(rec, reason):
   338	        excluded.append({"seq": rec["seq"], "reason": reason})
   339	
   340	    for rec in records:
   341	        if rec.get("event") != "run":
   342	            continue
   343	        if rec.get("phase") != "final":
   344	            exclude(rec, "phase!=final (%r)" % rec.get("phase"))
   345	            continue
   346	        if rec.get("exit") != "ok":
   347	            exclude(rec, "exit!=ok (%r)" % rec.get("exit"))
   348	            continue
   349	        if rec["seq"] in superseded:
   350	            exclude(rec, "superseded")
   351	            continue
   352	        if rec.get("prereg_hash") != frozen_sha:
   353	            exclude(rec, "prereg_hash mismatch")
   354	            continue
   355	        cfg = rec.get("config", {})
   356	        if seeds and "seed" in cfg and cfg["seed"] not in seeds:
   357	            exclude(rec, "seed %r not registered" % cfg["seed"])
   358	            continue
   359	        bad_level = None
   360	        for name, levels in iv_levels.items():
   361	            if name in cfg and cfg[name] not in levels:
   362	                bad_level = "config.%s=%r not in declared levels" % (name, cfg[name])
   363	                break
   364	        if bad_level:
   365	            exclude(rec, bad_level)
   366	            continue
   367	        eligible.append(rec)
   368	    return eligible, excluded
   369

   371	def confirmed_audit(root, exp_id, runner_ids):
   372	    """Return the path of a CONFIRMED audit by a non-runner identity, or None."""
   373	    for path in sorted(glob.glob(os.path.join(root, "registry", "audits", exp_id, "*.json"))):
   374	        with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   375	            audit = json.load(f)
   376	        if audit.get("outcome") != "CONFIRMED":
   377	            continue
   378	        auditor = audit.get("auditor")
   379	        if auditor in runner_ids:
   380	            raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_SELF_AUDIT",
   381	                              "%s: auditor %r also appears as a runner in the eligible log" % (path, auditor))
   382	        return os.path.relpath(path, root)
   383	    return None

   386	def main():
   387	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Compute an experiment verdict purely from frozen record + log.")
   388	    ap.add_argument("--experiment", required=True)
   389	    ap.add_argument("--agent-id", required=True, help="pseudonym stamped on the unblind log line")
   390	    ap.add_argument("--root", default=None)
   391	    ap.add_argument("--computed-at", default=None, help="UTC timestamp override (byte-determinism/tests)")
   392	    args = ap.parse_args()
   393	
   394	    root = args.root or os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   395	    exp_id = args.experiment
   396	    try:
   397	        run(root, exp_id, args.agent_id, args.computed_at)
   398	    except kc.KotError as e:
   399	        fail(e.code, str(e).split(": ", 1)[1])
   400	    except FileNotFoundError as e:
   401	        fail("ERR_P2_IO", str(e))
   402	
   403	
   404	def run(root, exp_id, agent_id, computed_at):
   405	    kc.require_pseudonym(agent_id, "--agent-id")
   406	
   407	    # Step 1 — frozen record integrity.
   408	    rec_path = os.path.join(root, "registry", "experiments", "%s.json" % exp_id)
   409	    index_path = os.path.join(root, "registry", "frozen-index.json")
   410	    with open(rec_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   411	        record = json.load(f)
   412	    with open(index_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   413	        index = json.load(f)
   414	    if exp_id not in index:
   415	        raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_NOT_FROZEN", "%s absent from frozen-index.json" % exp_id)
   416	    frozen_sha = index[exp_id]
   417	    if kc.frozen_hash(record) != frozen_sha or record.get("frozen_sha256") != frozen_sha:
   418	        raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_FROZEN_DRIFT",
   419	                          "%s: frozen record bytes drifted from frozen-index — no verdict is producible" % exp_id)
   420	
   421	    # Step 2 — amendment overlay (fail closed, never silently skipped). The
   422	    # chain-verified log is read first: it is the RT-5 cutoff witness. The
   423	    # overlay never touches the frozen file; `record` stays the frozen anchor
   424	    # (prereg_hash eligibility is checked against frozen_sha), `effective` is
   425	    # what readout runs against.
   426	    log_path = os.path.join(root, "results-log", "%s.jsonl" % exp_id)
   427	    records, raw_lines = kc.read_log(log_path)
   428	    effective, amendments_applied, amended_sha = apply_amendment_overlay(root, exp_id, record, records)
   429	
   430	    # Step 3 — eligibility.
   431	    eligible, excluded = select_eligible(records, frozen_sha, effective["design"])
   432	    tail_sha = kc.log_tail_sha256(raw_lines)
   433	    runner_ids = {r["runner"] for r in eligible}
   434	
   435	    # Step 3b — D9 reused-row eligibility (resource-optimization-plan.md §3
   436	    # revision-1). Reused producer rows enter the analysis ONLY when ALL hold:
   437	    #   - the frozen (effective) record is kot-reg/2 with a reused_from block
   438	    #     (an in-log reuse witness without a frozen declaration is refused);
   439	    #   - the ruling is maintainer-ratified (kc.check_record_reuse enforces);
   440	    #   - every RC-1..RC-8 machine check re-verifies at consumption time,
   441	    #     including RC-2 identity with placeholders now FILLED (no
   442	    #     indeterminate pins may license consumption);
   443	    #   - an in-chain RC-6 witness (event:"reuse") restates the exact verified
   444	    #     row set {seqs, row_hashes} for each block.
   445	    # Reused rows NEVER substitute for fresh evidence: the completeness gate
   446	    # below still requires >=1 fresh eligible run row.
   447	    reuse_witnesses = [r for r in records if r.get("event") == "reuse"]
   448	    reused_rows, reused_summary = [], []
   449	    if reuse_witnesses and not (effective.get("reused_from") or []):
   450	        raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_REUSE_UNDECLARED",
   451	                          "log carries %d reuse witness line(s) but the frozen record declares no "
   452	                          "reused_from block" % len(reuse_witnesses))
   453	    if effective.get("reused_from") and eligible:
   454	        for res in kc.check_record_reuse(effective, root, mode="verdict"):
   455	            block, rows, seqs = res["block"], res["rows"], res["seqs"]
   456	            verified = {rec_p["seq"]: kc.sha256_hex(raw_p) for rec_p, raw_p in rows}
   457	            witness_ok = False
   458	            for w in reuse_witnesses:
   459	                wr = w.get("reuse") or {}
   460	                if (wr.get("producer") == block["producer"]
   461	                        and wr.get("producer_frozen_sha256") == block["producer_frozen_sha256"]
   462	                        and dict(zip(wr.get("seqs", []), wr.get("row_hashes", []))) == verified):
   463	                    witness_ok = True
   464	                    break
   465	            if not witness_ok:
   466	                raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_REUSE_NO_WITNESS",
   467	                                  "no event:\"reuse\" line in the consumer log restates the verified "
   468	                                  "row set for producer %s (RC-6 in-chain witness; append it via "
   469	                                  "log-append before the verdict)" % block["producer"])
   470	            for rec_p, raw_p in rows:
   471	                aug = dict(rec_p)
   472	                aug["reuse_provenance"] = {
   473	                    "producer": block["producer"],
   474	                    "producer_frozen_sha256": block["producer_frozen_sha256"],
   475	                    "row_sha256": verified[rec_p["seq"]],
   476	                    "role": block["role"],
   477	                }
   478	                reused_rows.append(aug)
   479	            reused_summary.append({
   480	                "producer": block["producer"],
   481	                "producer_frozen_sha256": block["producer_frozen_sha256"],
   482	                "role": block["role"],
   483	                "seqs": seqs,
   484	                "rows_sha256": kc.canonical_sha256([verified[s] for s in seqs]),
   485	            })
   486	
   487	    verdict = None
   488	    fired_index = None
   489	    fired_rule = None
   490	    analysis = None
   491	    analysis_output_sha = None
   492	    # Ops amendments cannot touch /pins/analysis_script, so this pin is
   493	    # byte-identical between `record` and `effective`; read it from the
   494	    # effective record for uniformity.
   495	    script_sha = effective["pins"]["analysis_script"]["sha256"]
   496	    endpoint_results = []
   497	    coverage = None
   498	
   499	    if not eligible:
   500	        # Step 4 — completeness gate (minimal form).
   501	        verdict = "INCOMPLETE-DATA"
   502	    else:
   503	        # Step 5 — pinned analysis script over eligible records only.
   504	        script_path = os.path.join(root, effective["pins"]["analysis_script"]["path"])
   505	        got = file_sha256(script_path)
   506	        if got != script_sha:
   507	            raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_FROZEN_DRIFT",
   508	                              "pinned analysis script %s sha256 %s != frozen pin %s"
   509	                              % (effective["pins"]["analysis_script"]["path"], got, script_sha))
   510	        # Reused rows (verified above) follow the fresh eligible rows on stdin,
   511	        # each carrying reuse_provenance so the pinned analysis can distinguish
   512	        # fresh from reused (and apply the frozen selection-adjusted inference
   513	        # where RC-8 requires it).
   514	        stdin_payload = "".join(kc.canonical_dumps(r) + "\n" for r in eligible + reused_rows)
   515	        proc = subprocess.run(
   516	            ["nice", "-n", "10", sys.executable, script_path],
   517	            input=stdin_payload.encode("utf-8"), capture_output=True, cwd=root,
   518	        )
   519	        if proc.returncode != 0:
   520	            raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_ANALYSIS",
   521	                              "pinned analysis script exited %d: %s"
   522	                              % (proc.returncode, proc.stderr.decode("utf-8", "replace")[-2000:]))
   523	        try:
   524	            analysis = json.loads(proc.stdout.decode("utf-8"))
   525	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, UnicodeDecodeError) as e:
   526	            raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_ANALYSIS", "analysis output is not JSON: %s" % e)
   527	        out_dir = os.path.join(root, "reports", "auto", exp_id)
   528	        os.makedirs(out_dir, exist_ok=True)
   529	        out_path = os.path.join(out_dir, "analysis-output.json")
   530	        kc.write_canonical_json(out_path, analysis)
   531	        analysis_output_sha = file_sha256(out_path)
   532	
   533	        # Step 6 — unblind line, first time only, via the single write path.
   534	        if not any(r.get("event") == "unblind" for r in records):

   338	class MissingMetric(KotError):
   339	    def __init__(self, pointer):
   340	        self.pointer = pointer

   347	def resolve_pointer(doc, pointer):
   348	    """RFC-6901 JSON pointer; returns _MISSING sentinel when absent."""
   349	    if pointer == "":
   350	        return doc
   351	    if not pointer.startswith("/"):
   352	        return _MISSING
   353	    node = doc
   354	    for token in pointer[1:].split("/"):
   355	        token = token.replace("~1", "/").replace("~0", "~")
   356	        if isinstance(node, dict):
   357	            if token not in node:
   358	                return _MISSING
   359	            node = node[token]
   360	        elif isinstance(node, list):
   361	            if not re.match(r"^(0|[1-9][0-9]*)$", token) or int(token) >= len(node):
   362	                return _MISSING
   363	            node = node[int(token)]
   364	        else:
   365	            return _MISSING
   366	    return node
