   257	    schema_path = os.path.join(root, "registry", "schema", SCHEMA_FILES[sv])
   258	    with open(schema_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   259	        reg_schema = json.load(f)
   260	    errs = kc.validate_schema(effective, reg_schema)
   261	    if errs:
   262	        raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   263	                          "amended effective record no longer validates against %s: %s"
   264	                          % (sv, "; ".join(errs[:5])))
   265	    kc.check_identity_fields(effective)
   266	    hashed = {k: v for k, v in effective.items() if k not in ("status", "frozen_sha256")}
   267	    kc.require_no_account_strings(kc.canonical_bytes(hashed), "amended effective record")
   268	    return effective, applied, kc.frozen_hash(effective)
   269	
   270	
   271	# Scale-language license total order for the min-rule (G-12 vocabulary is the
   272	# established one across all 17 issued verdicts: none / sign-only / slope).
   273	SCALE_LICENSE_ORDER = {"none": 0, "sign-only": 1, "slope": 2}
   274	
   275	
   276	def scale_language_license(design, rungs, analysis):
   277	    """Compute scale_language_licensed with per-record slope enforcement.
   278	
   279	    The G-12 count map (docs/research-plan/02-data-and-reporting.md:664) is
   280	    UNCHANGED: 1 rung -> "none", 2 -> "sign-only", >=3 -> "slope"-ELIGIBLE.
   281	    What changed (a5-llm Gate-A re-audit REFUTE 2026-07-11, adjudicated in
   282	    docs/next/a5-llm-refute-adjudication.md section 2.1/2.2, maintainer-approved
   283	    2026-07-11 issue #5; correction record
   284	    registry/corrections/a5-llm/2-scale-license-erratum.json): a raw distinct-
   285	    rung-LABEL count used to license "slope" gate-blind and envelope-blind —
   286	    for a5-llm it counted the R0 baseline label as a rung and emitted "slope"
   287	    against a frozen envelope that says "never a slope law" and a scale-trend
   288	    instrument that failed. "slope" is therefore now emitted ONLY when ALL
   289	    THREE per-record caps allow it (min-rule, fail closed on any absent field
   290	    — explicitly NOT a blunt ">=4 rungs" rule, which would rewrite G-12 /
   291	    08-stats-and-extrapolation.md section 2.1: those license a scale-trend fit
   292	    at >=3 COMPARABLE rungs, and that stays licensable here):
   293	
   294	      (i)   design.scale_language_max == "slope" — the machine-readable frozen
   295	            PER-RECORD ceiling (the prose ceiling lives in
   296	            extrapolation_envelope_verbatim; this field is its machine-readable
   297	            form, set at freeze time). Absent field => no ceiling permits
   298	            slope => fail closed to "sign-only". A declared ceiling below the
   299	            count license also caps it (e.g. a declared "none" caps a 2-rung
   300	            "sign-only").
   301	      (ii)  >=3 measured rungs lie inside design.model_scale_rungs — the
   302	            frozen list of COMPARABLE MODEL-SCALE rung labels (08-stats
   303	            section 2.1 precondition; a baseline label like a5-llm's R0 must
   304	            never inflate the count).
   305	      (iii) every JSON pointer in design.scale_trend_valid_metrics resolves to
   306	            boolean true in the analysis output — the registered section-2
   307	            trend machinery actually RAN and produced a VALID result (for the
   308	            a5-llm shape: /gates/separation_valid plus the trend Holm member;
   309	            a trend member that left the family instrument-invalid can never
   310	            license a slope).
   311	
   312	    The 1-rung "none" and 2-rung "sign-only" paths are byte-identical to the
   313	    pre-fix generator, so every issued license-"none"/"sign-only" verdict
   314	    (f2b-replicate et al.) regenerates unchanged.
   315	    """
   316	    license_ = "none" if len(rungs) < 2 else ("sign-only" if len(rungs) == 2 else "slope")
   317	    ceiling = design.get("scale_language_max")
   318	    if license_ == "slope":
   319	        comparable = [r for r in rungs if r in (design.get("model_scale_rungs") or [])]
   320	        trend_ptrs = design.get("scale_trend_valid_metrics") or []
   321	        trend_valid = (bool(trend_ptrs) and analysis is not None
   322	                       and all(kc.resolve_pointer(analysis, p) is True for p in trend_ptrs))
   323	        if not (ceiling == "slope" and len(comparable) >= 3 and trend_valid):
   324	            license_ = "sign-only"
   325	    if ceiling in SCALE_LICENSE_ORDER and SCALE_LICENSE_ORDER[ceiling] < SCALE_LICENSE_ORDER[license_]:
   326	        license_ = ceiling
   327	    return license_
   328	
   329	
   330	def select_eligible(records, frozen_sha, design):
   331	    """P2 §3.1 step 3. Returns (eligible, excluded=[{seq, reason}])."""
   332	    superseded = {r.get("target_seq") for r in records if r.get("event") == "supersede"}
   333	    iv_levels = {iv["name"]: iv["levels"] for iv in design.get("independent_vars", [])}
   334	    seeds = design.get("seeds", [])
   335	    eligible, excluded = [], []
   336	
   337	    def exclude(rec, reason):
   338	        excluded.append({"seq": rec["seq"], "reason": reason})
   339	
   340	    for rec in records:
   341	        if rec.get("event") != "run":
   342	            continue
   343	        if rec.get("phase") != "final":
   344	            exclude(rec, "phase!=final (%r)" % rec.get("phase"))
   345	            continue
   346	        if rec.get("exit") != "ok":
   347	            exclude(rec, "exit!=ok (%r)" % rec.get("exit"))
   348	            continue
   349	        if rec["seq"] in superseded:
   350	            exclude(rec, "superseded")
   351	            continue
   352	        if rec.get("prereg_hash") != frozen_sha:
   353	            exclude(rec, "prereg_hash mismatch")
   354	            continue
   355	        cfg = rec.get("config", {})
   356	        if seeds and "seed" in cfg and cfg["seed"] not in seeds:
   357	            exclude(rec, "seed %r not registered" % cfg["seed"])
   358	            continue
   359	        bad_level = None
   360	        for name, levels in iv_levels.items():
   361	            if name in cfg and cfg[name] not in levels:
   362	                bad_level = "config.%s=%r not in declared levels" % (name, cfg[name])
   363	                break
   364	        if bad_level:
   365	            exclude(rec, bad_level)
   366	            continue
   367	        eligible.append(rec)
   368	    return eligible, excluded
   369	
   370	
   371	def confirmed_audit(root, exp_id, runner_ids):
   372	    """Return the path of a CONFIRMED audit by a non-runner identity, or None."""
   373	    for path in sorted(glob.glob(os.path.join(root, "registry", "audits", exp_id, "*.json"))):
   374	        with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   375	            audit = json.load(f)
   376	        if audit.get("outcome") != "CONFIRMED":
   377	            continue
   378	        auditor = audit.get("auditor")
   379	        if auditor in runner_ids:
   380	            raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_SELF_AUDIT",
   381	                              "%s: auditor %r also appears as a runner in the eligible log" % (path, auditor))
   382	        return os.path.relpath(path, root)
   383	    return None
   384	
   385	
   386	def main():
   387	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Compute an experiment verdict purely from frozen record + log.")
   388	    ap.add_argument("--experiment", required=True)
   389	    ap.add_argument("--agent-id", required=True, help="pseudonym stamped on the unblind log line")
   390	    ap.add_argument("--root", default=None)
   391	    ap.add_argument("--computed-at", default=None, help="UTC timestamp override (byte-determinism/tests)")
   392	    args = ap.parse_args()
   393	
   394	    root = args.root or os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   395	    exp_id = args.experiment
   396	    try:
   397	        run(root, exp_id, args.agent_id, args.computed_at)
   398	    except kc.KotError as e:
   399	        fail(e.code, str(e).split(": ", 1)[1])
   400	    except FileNotFoundError as e:
   401	        fail("ERR_P2_IO", str(e))
   402	
   403	
   404	def run(root, exp_id, agent_id, computed_at):
   405	    kc.require_pseudonym(agent_id, "--agent-id")
   406	
   407	    # Step 1 — frozen record integrity.
   408	    rec_path = os.path.join(root, "registry", "experiments", "%s.json" % exp_id)
   409	    index_path = os.path.join(root, "registry", "frozen-index.json")
   410	    with open(rec_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   411	        record = json.load(f)
   412	    with open(index_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   413	        index = json.load(f)
   414	    if exp_id not in index:
   415	        raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_NOT_FROZEN", "%s absent from frozen-index.json" % exp_id)
   416	    frozen_sha = index[exp_id]
   417	    if kc.frozen_hash(record) != frozen_sha or record.get("frozen_sha256") != frozen_sha:
   418	        raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_FROZEN_DRIFT",
   419	                          "%s: frozen record bytes drifted from frozen-index — no verdict is producible" % exp_id)
   420	
   421	    # Step 2 — amendment overlay (fail closed, never silently skipped). The
   422	    # chain-verified log is read first: it is the RT-5 cutoff witness. The
   423	    # overlay never touches the frozen file; `record` stays the frozen anchor
   424	    # (prereg_hash eligibility is checked against frozen_sha), `effective` is
   425	    # what readout runs against.
   426	    log_path = os.path.join(root, "results-log", "%s.jsonl" % exp_id)
   427	    records, raw_lines = kc.read_log(log_path)
   428	    effective, amendments_applied, amended_sha = apply_amendment_overlay(root, exp_id, record, records)
   429	
   430	    # Step 3 — eligibility.
   431	    eligible, excluded = select_eligible(records, frozen_sha, effective["design"])
   432	    tail_sha = kc.log_tail_sha256(raw_lines)
   433	    runner_ids = {r["runner"] for r in eligible}
   434	
   435	    # Step 3b — D9 reused-row eligibility (resource-optimization-plan.md §3
   436	    # revision-1). Reused producer rows enter the analysis ONLY when ALL hold:
   437	    #   - the frozen (effective) record is kot-reg/2 with a reused_from block
   438	    #     (an in-log reuse witness without a frozen declaration is refused);
   439	    #   - the ruling is maintainer-ratified (kc.check_record_reuse enforces);
   440	    #   - every RC-1..RC-8 machine check re-verifies at consumption time,
   441	    #     including RC-2 identity with placeholders now FILLED (no
   442	    #     indeterminate pins may license consumption);
   443	    #   - an in-chain RC-6 witness (event:"reuse") restates the exact verified
   444	    #     row set {seqs, row_hashes} for each block.
   445	    # Reused rows NEVER substitute for fresh evidence: the completeness gate
   446	    # below still requires >=1 fresh eligible run row.
   447	    reuse_witnesses = [r for r in records if r.get("event") == "reuse"]
   448	    reused_rows, reused_summary = [], []
   449	    if reuse_witnesses and not (effective.get("reused_from") or []):
   450	        raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_REUSE_UNDECLARED",
   451	                          "log carries %d reuse witness line(s) but the frozen record declares no "
   452	                          "reused_from block" % len(reuse_witnesses))
   453	    if effective.get("reused_from") and eligible:
   454	        for res in kc.check_record_reuse(effective, root, mode="verdict"):
   455	            block, rows, seqs = res["block"], res["rows"], res["seqs"]
   456	            verified = {rec_p["seq"]: kc.sha256_hex(raw_p) for rec_p, raw_p in rows}
   457	            witness_ok = False
   458	            for w in reuse_witnesses:
   459	                wr = w.get("reuse") or {}
   460	                if (wr.get("producer") == block["producer"]
   461	                        and wr.get("producer_frozen_sha256") == block["producer_frozen_sha256"]
   462	                        and dict(zip(wr.get("seqs", []), wr.get("row_hashes", []))) == verified):
   463	                    witness_ok = True
   464	                    break
   465	            if not witness_ok:
   466	                raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_REUSE_NO_WITNESS",
   467	                                  "no event:\"reuse\" line in the consumer log restates the verified "
   468	                                  "row set for producer %s (RC-6 in-chain witness; append it via "
   469	                                  "log-append before the verdict)" % block["producer"])
   470	            for rec_p, raw_p in rows:
   471	                aug = dict(rec_p)
   472	                aug["reuse_provenance"] = {
   473	                    "producer": block["producer"],
   474	                    "producer_frozen_sha256": block["producer_frozen_sha256"],
   475	                    "row_sha256": verified[rec_p["seq"]],
   476	                    "role": block["role"],
   477	                }
   478	                reused_rows.append(aug)
   479	            reused_summary.append({
   480	                "producer": block["producer"],
   481	                "producer_frozen_sha256": block["producer_frozen_sha256"],
   482	                "role": block["role"],
   483	                "seqs": seqs,
   484	                "rows_sha256": kc.canonical_sha256([verified[s] for s in seqs]),
   485	            })
   486	
   487	    verdict = None
   488	    fired_index = None
   489	    fired_rule = None
   490	    analysis = None
   491	    analysis_output_sha = None
   492	    # Ops amendments cannot touch /pins/analysis_script, so this pin is
   493	    # byte-identical between `record` and `effective`; read it from the
   494	    # effective record for uniformity.
   495	    script_sha = effective["pins"]["analysis_script"]["sha256"]
   496	    endpoint_results = []
   497	    coverage = None
   498	
   499	    if not eligible:
   500	        # Step 4 — completeness gate (minimal form).
   501	        verdict = "INCOMPLETE-DATA"
   502	    else:
   503	        # Step 5 — pinned analysis script over eligible records only.
   504	        script_path = os.path.join(root, effective["pins"]["analysis_script"]["path"])
   505	        got = file_sha256(script_path)
   506	        if got != script_sha:
   507	            raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_FROZEN_DRIFT",
   508	                              "pinned analysis script %s sha256 %s != frozen pin %s"
   509	                              % (effective["pins"]["analysis_script"]["path"], got, script_sha))
   510	        # Reused rows (verified above) follow the fresh eligible rows on stdin,
   511	        # each carrying reuse_provenance so the pinned analysis can distinguish
   512	        # fresh from reused (and apply the frozen selection-adjusted inference
   513	        # where RC-8 requires it).
   514	        stdin_payload = "".join(kc.canonical_dumps(r) + "\n" for r in eligible + reused_rows)
   515	        proc = subprocess.run(
   516	            ["nice", "-n", "10", sys.executable, script_path],
   517	            input=stdin_payload.encode("utf-8"), capture_output=True, cwd=root,
   518	        )
   519	        if proc.returncode != 0:
   520	            raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_ANALYSIS",
   521	                              "pinned analysis script exited %d: %s"
   522	                              % (proc.returncode, proc.stderr.decode("utf-8", "replace")[-2000:]))
   523	        try:
   524	            analysis = json.loads(proc.stdout.decode("utf-8"))
   525	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, UnicodeDecodeError) as e:
   526	            raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_ANALYSIS", "analysis output is not JSON: %s" % e)
   527	        out_dir = os.path.join(root, "reports", "auto", exp_id)
   528	        os.makedirs(out_dir, exist_ok=True)
   529	        out_path = os.path.join(out_dir, "analysis-output.json")
   530	        kc.write_canonical_json(out_path, analysis)
   531	        analysis_output_sha = file_sha256(out_path)
   532	
   533	        # Step 6 — unblind line, first time only, via the single write path.
   534	        if not any(r.get("event") == "unblind" for r in records):
   535	            unblind = {"event": "unblind", "prereg_hash": frozen_sha}
   536	            p = subprocess.run(
   537	                [sys.executable, os.path.join(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)), "log-append.py"),
   538	                 "--experiment", exp_id, "--agent-id", agent_id, "--record", "-", "--root", root],
   539	                input=kc.canonical_dumps(unblind).encode("utf-8"), capture_output=True,
   540	            )
   541	            if p.returncode != 0:
   542	                raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_CHAIN", "could not write unblind line: %s"
   543	                                  % p.stderr.decode("utf-8", "replace"))
   544	            records, raw_lines = kc.read_log(log_path)
   545	            tail_sha = kc.log_tail_sha256(raw_lines)
   546	
   547	        # G-7 (scoped): coverage is required only when the frozen record declares it.
   548	        # coverage_requirement.source names WHERE the coverage block comes from
   549	        # (P2 §2 G-7: "absent from eligible runs' metrics or the verdict object"):
   550	        #   - "self" / this experiment's id: the experiment measures its own
   551	        #     coverage and every eligible run carries metrics.coverage (m0b).
   552	        #   - any other experiment id: the published coverage from that
   553	        #     experiment's verdict object is RESTATED here (P3 m0b.close:
   554	        #     "publish coverage + rung; restated in every later verdict").
   555	        #     Fail closed unless that verdict exists, carries a coverage
   556	        #     block, and is itself audit-CONFIRMED (G-6 — an unaudited
   557	        #     coverage number must not silently license downstream verdicts).
   558	        if "coverage_requirement" in effective:
   559	            cov_source = effective["coverage_requirement"].get("source", "self")
   560	            if cov_source in ("self", exp_id):
   561	                coverage = eligible[-1].get("metrics", {}).get("coverage")
   562	                if not coverage:
   563	                    raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_NO_COVERAGE",
   564	                                      "coverage_requirement declared but eligible runs carry no metrics.coverage block")
   565	            else:
   566	                src_path = os.path.join(root, "registry", "verdicts", "%s.json" % cov_source)
   567	                if not os.path.exists(src_path):
   568	                    raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_NO_COVERAGE",
   569	                                      "coverage_requirement.source=%r but registry/verdicts/%s.json does not exist"
   570	                                      % (cov_source, cov_source))
   571	                with open(src_path, encoding="utf-8") as f:
   572	                    src_verdict = json.load(f)
   573	                coverage = src_verdict.get("coverage")
   574	                if not coverage:
   575	                    raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_NO_COVERAGE",
   576	                                      "coverage_requirement.source=%r but that verdict object carries no coverage block"
   577	                                      % cov_source)
   578	                if src_verdict.get("audit", {}).get("state") != "CONFIRMED":
   579	                    raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_NO_COVERAGE",
   580	                                      "coverage_requirement.source=%r but that verdict's audit state is %r, not CONFIRMED"
   581	                                      % (cov_source, src_verdict.get("audit", {}).get("state")))
   582	
   583	        for ep in effective["endpoints"]:
   584	            v = kc.resolve_pointer(analysis, ep["metric"])
   585	            endpoint_results.append({
   586	                "id": ep["id"], "role": ep["role"], "metric": ep["metric"],
   587	                "value": None if v is kc._MISSING else v,
   588	            })
   589	
   590	        # Step 7 — frozen verdict rules, first match wins; fail closed on gaps.
   591	        try:
   592	            for i, rule in enumerate(effective["verdict_rules"]):
   593	                if kc.eval_expr(rule["when"], analysis):
   594	                    verdict = rule["verdict"]
   595	                    fired_index = i
   596	                    fired_rule = rule["when"]
   597	                    break
   598	        except kc.MissingMetric as e:
   599	            verdict = "INCOMPLETE-DATA"
   600	            fired_rule = {"missing_metric": e.pointer}
   601	        if verdict is None:
   602	            # unreachable when the freeze-time catch-all lint held; fail closed anyway
   603	            verdict = "INCOMPLETE-DATA"
   604	
   605	    # Step 8 — audit gate on PASS.
   606	    audit_state = {"state": "N/A", "path": None}
   607	    if verdict == "PASS":
   608	        audit_path = confirmed_audit(root, exp_id, runner_ids)
   609	        if audit_path is None:
   610	            verdict = "PASS-PENDING-AUDIT"
   611	            audit_state = {"state": "PENDING", "path": None}
   612	        else:
   613	            audit_state = {"state": "CONFIRMED", "path": audit_path}
   614	
   615	    rungs_fresh = sorted({str(r["config"]["rung"]) for r in eligible
   616	                          if isinstance(r.get("config"), dict) and "rung" in r["config"]})
   617	    rungs_reused = sorted({str(r["config"]["rung"]) for r in reused_rows
   618	                           if isinstance(r.get("config"), dict) and "rung" in r["config"]})
   619	    rungs = sorted(set(rungs_fresh) | set(rungs_reused))
   620	    # Per-record min-rule licensing (a5-llm REFUTE remediation) — see
   621	    # scale_language_license's docstring for the three caps and their sources.
   622	    license_ = scale_language_license(effective["design"], rungs, analysis)
   623	
   624	    verdict_obj = {
   625	        "schema_version": "kot-verdict/1",
   626	        "experiment": exp_id,
   627	        "hypotheses": effective["hypotheses"],
   628	        "verdict": verdict,
   629	        "fired_rule_index": fired_index,
   630	        "fired_rule": fired_rule,
   631	        "computed_at": computed_at or datetime.datetime.utcnow().strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),
   632	        "inputs": {
   633	            "frozen_sha256": frozen_sha,
   634	            "amendments_applied": amendments_applied,
   635	            "amended_record_sha256": amended_sha,
   636	            "log_tail_sha256": tail_sha,
   637	            "eligible_runs": len(eligible),
   638	            "excluded_runs": excluded,
   639	            "analysis_output_sha256": analysis_output_sha,
   640	            "analysis_script_sha256": script_sha,
   641	        },
   642	        "endpoint_results": endpoint_results,
   643	        "coverage": coverage,
   644	        "rungs_measured": rungs,
   645	        "scale_language_licensed": license_,
   646	        # (fields below added only for reuse-consuming records, so pre-D9
   647	        # verdict objects keep their exact shape)
   648	        "kill_criterion_verbatim": effective["kill_criterion_verbatim"],
   649	        "extrapolation_envelope_verbatim": effective["extrapolation_envelope_verbatim"],
   650	        "audit": audit_state,
   651	    }
   652	    if reused_summary:
   653	        # Transparency for the auditor (RC-6): what was consumed, from where,
   654	        # and which rungs the record measured fresh vs inherited via reuse —
   655	        # any scale-language license that leans on reused rungs is visible.
   656	        verdict_obj["inputs"]["reused"] = reused_summary
   657	        verdict_obj["rungs_fresh"] = rungs_fresh
   658	        verdict_obj["rungs_reused_only"] = sorted(set(rungs_reused) - set(rungs_fresh))
   659

   338	class MissingMetric(KotError):
   339	    def __init__(self, pointer):
   340	        self.pointer = pointer
   341	        super().__init__("ERR_P2_MISSING_METRIC", "pointer %r missing/null in analysis output" % pointer)
   342	
   343	
   344	_MISSING = object()
   345	
   346	
   347	def resolve_pointer(doc, pointer):
   348	    """RFC-6901 JSON pointer; returns _MISSING sentinel when absent."""
   349	    if pointer == "":
   350	        return doc
   351	    if not pointer.startswith("/"):
   352	        return _MISSING
   353	    node = doc
   354	    for token in pointer[1:].split("/"):
   355	        token = token.replace("~1", "/").replace("~0", "~")
   356	        if isinstance(node, dict):
   357	            if token not in node:
   358	                return _MISSING
   359	            node = node[token]
   360	        elif isinstance(node, list):
   361	            if not re.match(r"^(0|[1-9][0-9]*)$", token) or int(token) >= len(node):
   362	                return _MISSING
   363	            node = node[int(token)]
   364	        else:
   365	            return _MISSING
   366	    return node

docs/next/a5-llm-refute-adjudication.md:159:The 2026-07-11 Gate-A re-audit's REFUTE is correct against the record-as-frozen but changes no science: the auditor reproduced every verdict-bearing number of a5-llm to full precision (primary 0.6602, cost ratio 2.3×10⁴, all gates and Holm outcomes, log chain, frozen hash) and failed only pin verification and scale-language licensing. It is the THIRD audit of this record (2026-07-09 REJECT on the pin; 2026-07-10 CONFIRM with correction/1 in scope but no scale-language check; 2026-07-11 REFUTE), and it invalidates the completeness of the intervening CONFIRM — the ledger's `codex_audited: CONFIRM` is stale, no formal kot-audit/1 record or audit-ref line exists for any of the three, and reconciliation is a named action (#4). Defect 1 (kot_axiom.py pin `d2064989` vs executed `b6226940`) is the identical, hash-for-hash defect already lawfully adjudicated in `registry/corrections/a5-llm/1` as a freeze-time stale-hash clerical error, benign for every measured endpoint — I independently re-derived all four hashes from git and the measured outcome-inertness (`engine_matches_a5=1`, per-query identical) — so it needs no new correction, only a re-audit run WITH the erratum in scope, which correction/1 itself pre-declared as the path to CONFIRMED. Defect 2 is new and real, but its law reading matters: programme law does NOT cap 3 rungs at sign-only (08-stats §2.1 licenses a trend fit at ≥3 comparable rungs; G-12 maps ≥3 → slope) — the defect is PER-RECORD: verdict-gen.py:560 counted a non-model-scale rung label (R0), ignored §2.1's preconditions (comparable rungs + a validly produced trend result — a5-llm's trend instrument failed), and ignored the frozen envelope's explicit "never a slope law". The minimal fix is per-record enforcement — min(machine-readable frozen envelope ceiling, G-12 count over comparable model-scale rungs, valid-trend-result) — NOT a global ≥4-rung rule, which would be a maintainer-level law change this document does not propose. Issued blast radius: exactly two records — a5-llm (must read `sign-only`) and the already-FAILed f2 (envelope-capped to direction-only; its three rungs are genuine model-scale rungs, a different failure mode). With remediation in the review's order — maintainer rulings on per-record enforcement and on the documented S7 verdict-regeneration path (the two genuinely maintainer-gated items), correction/2, generator fix, registry reconciliation, corrections-in-scope re-audit, then upgrade under the settled policy — a5-llm's PASS-PENDING-AUDIT is numerically supportable and expected to reach CONFIRMED, though it is not confirmed today. What survives is deliberately narrow: a marginal-inference-cost substrate comparison on formal, self-authored queries against the engine's own typed store — robust from 0.660 as-logged through 0.577 complete-case down to a 0.4626 floor when every truncated best-cell row is credited correct — a sign at R1–R3 SmolLM2 only; not verifier-offload evidence generally, and never a trend.

registry/assessments/truthstyle-2x2.json:74:  "composition_note": "Where this sits in the correctness-thesis evidence tree. The thesis currently rests on two live lines plus their instruments: (1) the f2b line (verifier-offload): f2b-replicate PROMOTED at envelope scope; f2b-transfer frozen and unconsumed (the sole H-TRANSFER vs H-CIRC adjudicator); f2b-transfer-llmproxy A_1p=0.95 as a weak feasibility proxy (ASM-0022 ceiling); (2) the NL-boundary line (l3a-parse / a5-llm): grammar reachability and the engine-vs-LLM differentiator, with its own audit custody. truthstyle-2x2 is f2b-line-LOCAL: it is a NECESSARY SUPPORTING result - a style-robustness certificate for the judge instrument - not a direct kernel-correctness claim. Necessary in the precise sense that without it every f2b-line LLM-judge endorsement statistic carried an unquantified style channel (the guard this record's consumer rule governed), and the llmproxy assessment could cite A_1p=0.95 only with that channel explicitly open; with it, the channel is bounded at 0.10 (measured 0.025) for the pool actually in force. Supporting in the precise sense that it scores zero kernel content: were every kernel gloss wrong, this record could read identically (a style-robust judge is an instrument fact). It contributes to the CORRECTNESS thesis by making the f2b line's endorsement evidence INTERPRETABLE, and it was one of the three pre-unblind obligations the llmproxy assessment named (truthstyle-2x2; knull + content-injection map; failure-cause taxonomy) - it has now landed WITH confirmatory standing, having frozen 2026-07-10T10:02:06Z before f2b-transfer Stage-2 unblinds (R-2 timing honoured). It neither strengthens nor constrains the NL-boundary records: l3a/a5 consume no LLM-judge endorsement reading of kernel-register text, so the guard-lift does not reach them and their results do not back-stop this one. The load-bearing chain for the eventual correctness verdict now reads: judge instrument style-clean at margin (THIS RECORD) -> proxy endorsement interpretable (llmproxy, weak-proxy ceiling) -> human Stage-1 adjudicates H-TRANSFER vs H-CIRC (still guarded for humans, ASM-0047) -> knull adjudicates what any lift is evidence OF. Only the last two move the kernel-content claim itself.",

registry/assessments/nsk1-bprime2.json:78:      "recommended": "STAGE-1 FREE-GENERATION KEYED-RESCUE PROBE FIRST (rank 1), then the redesigned DRAFT record. The designer should spec a bounded successor at cells (12,16) and (16,16) only (multiplicity 2, no new grid search): keyed emission + rescue-of-text-only-failures under real vs deranged-coin arms in free generation, an alpha ladder BELOW 1.0 (the keying-vs-damage frontier - does keying survive at alpha where the 13-16% plus-sign breakage does not?), >= 2 additional derangement seeds (single-seed robustness is the cheapest remaining attack), and the role arm carried as covariate. Same instrument family, same caps class (~USD 1-5 at measured throughput), phase:exploratory, fork-deciding only. WHY THIS ORDER: a rescue-positive makes the redesigned nsk1 record's rung logic and power real instead of extrapolated from margins; a rescue-negative (content readable but never integrated in generation) redirects Stage-1 toward read-out placement rather than a doomed campaign - either way the DRAFT record is written once, on measurements.",

The violation rates here ARE two LLMs' blind judgments of ordinary usage and literal condition satisfaction — NOT native-speaker human competence, which is the HS3 estimand. Binding weakenings: (1) ESTIMAND GAP — LLM ordinary-usage and literal-reading judgments approximate human judgments with unproven systematic offsets (ASM-0532); the pass-B literalism channel can push the necessity rate UP (hyper-literal condition failures) or DOWN (charitable reading), direction unproven, so NEITHER verdict direction is a measurement of the human rates. (2) PAIR, NOT PANEL — the cross-family pair is stronger than a single-family pair (kappa carries cross-family diagnostic content) but both judges are web-scale-trained LLMs with shared exposure to ordinary-language usage data; kappa is judge-pair stability, never inter-HUMAN-annotator agreement evidence; judge-pB's vendor family overlaps the materials' authoring agents (disclosed). (5) LICENSE OF A PASS — a PASS-analog means exactly: 'a blind cross-family LLM pair, under the frozen two-pass rendering with working escapes, finds union-rule necessity violations at <= the 0.10 Wilson bar on the 200 pinned instances'. carries no verdict weight"}],"extrapolation_envelope_verbatim":"Binding on ANY verdict of this record and on every citation of its numbers. STAND-IN, NOT THE ADJUDICATING STUDY: the two-independent-human g3.annotate role (GATE-H, ~8-10h, blind to hypotheses) is filled by a pinned cross-family LLM pair (judge-pA GPT-5.6-Sol via npx-pinned codex-cli 0.144.1 effort low; judge-pB Claude Haiku 4.5 via headless claude -p under the truthstyle-2x2 section-4.3 hardening) because the human annotators are unsourced at run time (open decision O-3); g3 (frozen_sha256 ef9608c60fc0355074b2d50132bbc79cbe49c5e9b578c724d81d31ce95bef7aa) remains FROZEN, unconsumed, and is the ONLY experiment on this line whose readout adjudicates HS3 or triggers the HS2 sidecar auto-resolution and g5 pruning. The violation rates here ARE two LLMs' blind judgments of ordinary usage and literal condition satisfaction — NOT native-speaker human competence, which is the HS3 estimand. Binding weakenings: (1) ESTIMAND GAP — LLM ordinary-usage and literal-reading judgments approximate human judgments with unproven systematic offsets (ASM-0532); the pass-B literalism channel can push the necessity rate UP (hyper-literal condition failures) or DOWN (charitable reading), direction unproven, so NEITHER verdict direction is a measurement of the human rates. (2) PAIR, NOT PANEL — the cross-family pair is stronger than a single-family pair (kappa carries cross-family diagnostic content) but both judges are web-scale-trained LLMs with shared exposure to ordinary-language usage data; kappa is judge-pair stability, never inter-HUMAN-annotator agreement evidence; judge-pB's vendor family overlaps the materials' authoring agents (disclosed). (3) BRACKET RULE, NOT THE HUMAN COMBINATION RULE — the human record's combination rule is deliberately open; this record's pre-declared concordant/union bracket (ASM-0531) makes each verdict direction conservative under ITS least favourable rule, and the eventual human round is free to fix its own rule — the proxy consumes no open decision. (4) TRADITION FAMILIARITY — both judge families plausibly contain NSM-adjacent literature (ASM-0552); the mismatched-conditions probe bounds only crude non-literal endorsement. (5) LICENSE OF A PASS — a PASS-analog means exactly: 'a blind cross-family LLM pair, under the frozen two-pass rendering with working escapes, finds union-rule necessity violations at <= the 0.10 Wilson bar on the 200 pinned instances'. It is a WEAK FEASIBILITY PROXY (ASM-0550). Question: Is `urn:kotw:v0:code-fn-kot-common--canonical-dumps` an instance of the concept `urn:kot:bciqle4alzkancizxcexlnn76y47irpfvkzbknhsj6kxig266zyyahba`?","role":"user"}],"n_context_records":192,"prompt_chars":20119,"qid":"a0667"}
   150	- **PROPOSED-ASM-A (scale-license reliability):** "`scale_language_licensed` in kot-verdict/1 objects generated by tools/registry/verdict-gen.py (and -dts) BEFORE the §2.2 fix is unreliable whenever ≥3 distinct rung labels appear in eligible runs; the binding license for any such record is min(frozen envelope verbatim ceiling, G-12 count over COMPARABLE MODEL-SCALE rungs per 08-stats §2.1, validity of the registered trend machinery's realized result). Affected issued records: a5-llm (slope→sign-only), f2 (slope→direction-only per HE1). All license-'none' records unaffected against this failure mode." Expires when action #3 lands + the two errata are issued.
   151	- **PROPOSED-ASM-B (audit scope):** "Gate-A audits take registry/corrections/<experiment>/ as mandatory in-scope inputs; a literal pin-verification failure whose hashes match an in-scope adjudicated erratum is reported as 'frozen-record defect confirmed; corrected reading verified', not as a standalone REFUTE ground. The auditor independently re-verifies the erratum's hash claims." Expires when action #7's protocol text lands.
   152	- **PROPOSED-ASM-C (live-tree drift):** "tools/axiom/kot_axiom.py has changed again post-a5-llm (live sha d26408815238…, commit bfc00115); audits and regressions for a5/a5-llm verify engine identity against results-log `pins_observed` + git history, never against the working tree." Standing until an engine-pinning convention supersedes it.
   153	- **PROPOSED-ASM-D (stale audit ledger):** "registry/audit-status.jsonl's a5-llm `codex_audited: CONFIRM` records the 2026-07-10 audit only and is STALE: the 2026-07-11 REFUTE invalidates that confirmation's completeness (its checklist had no scale-language item). No kot-audit/1 record under registry/audits/a5-llm/ and no audit-ref log line exists for any of the three audits. a5-llm is PASS-PENDING-AUDIT, not confirmed, until actions #4–#6 complete; downstream documents (incl. docs/next/feasibility-synthesis.md:84,143) must not cite the ledger CONFIRM as current." Expires when actions #4–#6 land.
   154	
   155	---
   156	
   157	## Executive summary
   158	
   159	The 2026-07-11 Gate-A re-audit's REFUTE is correct against the record-as-frozen but changes no science: the auditor reproduced every verdict-bearing number of a5-llm to full precision (primary 0.6602, cost ratio 2.3×10⁴, all gates and Holm outcomes, log chain, frozen hash) and failed only pin verification and scale-language licensing. It is the THIRD audit of this record (2026-07-09 REJECT on the pin; 2026-07-10 CONFIRM with correction/1 in scope but no scale-language check; 2026-07-11 REFUTE), and it invalidates the completeness of the intervening CONFIRM — the ledger's `codex_audited: CONFIRM` is stale, no formal kot-audit/1 record or audit-ref line exists for any of the three, and reconciliation is a named action (#4). Defect 1 (kot_axiom.py pin `d2064989` vs executed `b6226940`) is the identical, hash-for-hash defect already lawfully adjudicated in `registry/corrections/a5-llm/1` as a freeze-time stale-hash clerical error, benign for every measured endpoint — I independently re-derived all four hashes from git and the measured outcome-inertness (`engine_matches_a5=1`, per-query identical) — so it needs no new correction, only a re-audit run WITH the erratum in scope, which correction/1 itself pre-declared as the path to CONFIRMED. Defect 2 is new and real, but its law reading matters: programme law does NOT cap 3 rungs at sign-only (08-stats §2.1 licenses a trend fit at ≥3 comparable rungs; G-12 maps ≥3 → slope) — the defect is PER-RECORD: verdict-gen.py:560 counted a non-model-scale rung label (R0), ignored §2.1's preconditions (comparable rungs + a validly produced trend result — a5-llm's trend instrument failed), and ignored the frozen envelope's explicit "never a slope law". The minimal fix is per-record enforcement — min(machine-readable frozen envelope ceiling, G-12 count over comparable model-scale rungs, valid-trend-result) — NOT a global ≥4-rung rule, which would be a maintainer-level law change this document does not propose. Issued blast radius: exactly two records — a5-llm (must read `sign-only`) and the already-FAILed f2 (envelope-capped to direction-only; its three rungs are genuine model-scale rungs, a different failure mode). With remediation in the review's order — maintainer rulings on per-record enforcement and on the documented S7 verdict-regeneration path (the two genuinely maintainer-gated items), correction/2, generator fix, registry reconciliation, corrections-in-scope re-audit, then upgrade under the settled policy — a5-llm's PASS-PENDING-AUDIT is numerically supportable and expected to reach CONFIRMED, though it is not confirmed today. What survives is deliberately narrow: a marginal-inference-cost substrate comparison on formal, self-authored queries against the engine's own typed store — robust from 0.660 as-logged through 0.577 complete-case down to a 0.4626 floor when every truncated best-cell row is credited correct — a sign at R1–R3 SmolLM2 only; not verifier-offload evidence generally, and never a trend.

item.text: One further statistical defect appears in the helper itself: it computes the initial G-12 tier from all labels, then uses comparable-rung count only as a Boolean slope gate. Thus three raw labels with only zero or one comparable model-scale rung are downgraded merely to `sign-only`, whereas the approved minimum rule over comparable rungs requires `none`. I’m doing a final issued-record sweep and line-level evidence pass before reporting.

/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import glob,json,importlib.util
spec=importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('vg','tools/registry/verdict-gen.py'); vg=importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(vg)
for p in sorted(glob.glob('registry/verdicts/*.json')):
  with open(p) as f:v=json.load(f)
  ep=v['experiment']; rp='registry/experiments/'+ep+'.json'
  if not __import__('os').path.exists(rp): continue
  with open(rp) as f:r=json.load(f)
  old=v.get('scale_language_licensed'); new=vg.scale_language_license(r['design'],v.get('rungs_measured',[]),{})
  fields=[k for k in ('scale_language_max','model_scale_rungs','scale_trend_valid_metrics') if k in r['design']]
  print(ep, len(v.get('rungs_measured',[])), old, new, fields)
print('counterexample',vg.scale_language_license({'scale_language_max':'slope','model_scale_rungs':['R1'],'scale_trend_valid_metrics':['/ok']},['BASE0','BASE1','R1'],{'ok':True}))

   271	# Scale-language license total order for the min-rule (G-12 vocabulary is the
   272	# established one across all 17 issued verdicts: none / sign-only / slope).
   273	SCALE_LICENSE_ORDER = {"none": 0, "sign-only": 1, "slope": 2}
   274	
   275	
   276	def scale_language_license(design, rungs, analysis):
   277	    """Compute scale_language_licensed with per-record slope enforcement.
   278	
   279	    The G-12 count map (docs/research-plan/02-data-and-reporting.md:664) is
   280	    UNCHANGED: 1 rung -> "none", 2 -> "sign-only", >=3 -> "slope"-ELIGIBLE.
   281	    What changed (a5-llm Gate-A re-audit REFUTE 2026-07-11, adjudicated in
   282	    docs/next/a5-llm-refute-adjudication.md section 2.1/2.2, maintainer-approved
   283	    2026-07-11 issue #5; correction record
   284	    registry/corrections/a5-llm/2-scale-license-erratum.json): a raw distinct-
   285	    rung-LABEL count used to license "slope" gate-blind and envelope-blind —
   286	    for a5-llm it counted the R0 baseline label as a rung and emitted "slope"
   287	    against a frozen envelope that says "never a slope law" and a scale-trend
   288	    instrument that failed. "slope" is therefore now emitted ONLY when ALL
   289	    THREE per-record caps allow it (min-rule, fail closed on any absent field
   290	    — explicitly NOT a blunt ">=4 rungs" rule, which would rewrite G-12 /
   291	    08-stats-and-extrapolation.md section 2.1: those license a scale-trend fit
   292	    at >=3 COMPARABLE rungs, and that stays licensable here):
   293	
   294	      (i)   design.scale_language_max == "slope" — the machine-readable frozen
   295	            PER-RECORD ceiling (the prose ceiling lives in
   296	            extrapolation_envelope_verbatim; this field is its machine-readable
   297	            form, set at freeze time). Absent field => no ceiling permits
   298	            slope => fail closed to "sign-only". A declared ceiling below the
   299	            count license also caps it (e.g. a declared "none" caps a 2-rung
   300	            "sign-only").
   301	      (ii)  >=3 measured rungs lie inside design.model_scale_rungs — the
   302	            frozen list of COMPARABLE MODEL-SCALE rung labels (08-stats
   303	            section 2.1 precondition; a baseline label like a5-llm's R0 must
   304	            never inflate the count).
   305	      (iii) every JSON pointer in design.scale_trend_valid_metrics resolves to
   306	            boolean true in the analysis output — the registered section-2
   307	            trend machinery actually RAN and produced a VALID result (for the
   308	            a5-llm shape: /gates/separation_valid plus the trend Holm member;
   309	            a trend member that left the family instrument-invalid can never
   310	            license a slope).
   311	
   312	    The 1-rung "none" and 2-rung "sign-only" paths are byte-identical to the
   313	    pre-fix generator, so every issued license-"none"/"sign-only" verdict
   314	    (f2b-replicate et al.) regenerates unchanged.
   315	    """
   316	    license_ = "none" if len(rungs) < 2 else ("sign-only" if len(rungs) == 2 else "slope")
   317	    ceiling = design.get("scale_language_max")
   318	    if license_ == "slope":
   319	        comparable = [r for r in rungs if r in (design.get("model_scale_rungs") or [])]
   320	        trend_ptrs = design.get("scale_trend_valid_metrics") or []
   321	        trend_valid = (bool(trend_ptrs) and analysis is not None
   322	                       and all(kc.resolve_pointer(analysis, p) is True for p in trend_ptrs))
   323	        if not (ceiling == "slope" and len(comparable) >= 3 and trend_valid):
   324	            license_ = "sign-only"
   325	    if ceiling in SCALE_LICENSE_ORDER and SCALE_LICENSE_ORDER[ceiling] < SCALE_LICENSE_ORDER[license_]:
   326	        license_ = ceiling
   327	    return license_
   328	
   615	    rungs_fresh = sorted({str(r["config"]["rung"]) for r in eligible
   616	                          if isinstance(r.get("config"), dict) and "rung" in r["config"]})
   617	    rungs_reused = sorted({str(r["config"]["rung"]) for r in reused_rows
   618	                           if isinstance(r.get("config"), dict) and "rung" in r["config"]})
   619	    rungs = sorted(set(rungs_fresh) | set(rungs_reused))
   620	    # Per-record min-rule licensing (a5-llm REFUTE remediation) — see
   621	    # scale_language_license's docstring for the three caps and their sources.
   622	    license_ = scale_language_license(effective["design"], rungs, analysis)

item.text:
The remediation is **not CONFIRMED**. The two errata are sound and frozen bytes are unchanged, but the forward generator remediation is incomplete and has a residual licensing defect.

1. The a5-llm erratum is correct.

[2-scale-license-erratum.json](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/registry/corrections/a5-llm/2-scale-license-erratum.json:12) accurately identifies the raw-label-count defect. Lines 13–19 document all three relevant facts: R0 is not a model-scale rung, the frozen envelope says “never a slope law,” and both `separation_valid` and `scale_trend_rag` are false. Lines 24–28 bind `slope` → `sign-only` without changing measured results. The maintainer approval is cited at line 8.

The cited hashes for the verdict and analysis output reproduce. The a5-llm experiment, log, issued verdict, and frozen index have no diff from `HEAD`; the issued verdict remains SHA-256 `f893cf47…`.

2. The f2 erratum is also correct.

[f2/2-scale-license-erratum.json](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/registry/corrections/f2/2-scale-license-erratum.json:12) correctly distinguishes f2 from a5-llm: its three rungs are genuinely comparable, but its envelope is direction-only and no valid fitted scale trend exists. Lines 21–26 bind the established verdict vocabulary to `sign-only`; lines 29–32 correctly note that the `FAIL` verdict and all measured results remain unchanged. Approval is cited at line 8.

The f2 frozen record and verdict also match `HEAD` byte-for-byte; the verdict hash remains `38422b3c…`.

3. `verdict-gen.py` prevents the reported a5-llm over-license, but does not fully implement the approved minimum rule.

The positive part is correct: [verdict-gen.py](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/tools/registry/verdict-gen.py:318) permits `slope` only when the ceiling is `slope`, at least three measured labels occur in `model_scale_rungs`, and every declared trend-validity pointer resolves to Boolean `true` (lines 318–324). Exactly three comparable rungs can still produce `slope`, so this is not a blunt ≥4 rule.

However, lines 316–324 initially compute the G-12 tier from **all raw rung labels**, then use comparable-rung count only as a slope/no-slope Boolean. This is not the documented minimum rule over comparable rungs. For example:

```text
measured = BASE0, BASE1, R1
model_scale_rungs = R1
ceiling = slope
valid trend = true
```

The implementation returns `sign-only`; G-12 over the single comparable rung requires `none`. Thus non-model labels can still inflate `none` to `sign-only`. The tests do not cover this zero/one-comparable-rung case.

The issued-record sweep nevertheless confirms that all existing zero-, one-, and two-rung verdict licenses remain unchanged. Only a5-llm and f2 move from `slope` to `sign-only` under the helper.

4. The machine-readable frozen controls cannot currently pass the normal freeze path.

Both registry schemas set `design.additionalProperties: false` and omit all three new fields: [kot-reg-1.json](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/registry/schema/kot-reg-1.json:70) and [kot-reg-2.json](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/registry/schema/kot-reg-2.json:70). Schema validation rejects:

```text
design.scale_language_max
design.model_scale_rungs
design.scale_trend_valid_metrics
```

There is also no freeze-time validation that the machine-readable ceiling agrees with the binding envelope, despite the approved requirement in [a5-llm-refute-adjudication.md](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/a5-llm-refute-adjudication.md:90).

The test file explicitly acknowledges and bypasses this defect by “hand freezing” invalid fixtures at [test_verdict_gen_scale.py](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/tools/registry/test_verdict_gen_scale.py:217). Consequently, its preservation test proves that the helper can return `slope`, but not that a lawfully preregistered record can reach that path.

5. The tests only partially establish the requested claims.

The real a5-llm check at [test_verdict_gen_scale.py](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/tools/registry/test_verdict_gen_scale.py:83) calls the helper directly; it does not regenerate a5-llm through the real CLI. The CLI regression at line 274 uses a synthetic, hand-frozen fixture. The three-rung preservation case at line 301 and four-rung ceiling case at line 318 are otherwise correctly specified.

Twelve pure-function/real-artifact tests passed. Five CLI tests could not execute in this read-only audit environment because no writable temporary directory was available; no assertion failed.

6. Two approved forward-path edits are missing.

The adjudication explicitly calls for fixing both generators, but [verdict-gen-dts.py](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/tools/registry/verdict-gen-dts.py:272) still emits `slope` solely from raw rung count. [report-gen.py](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/tools/registry/report-gen.py:192) also still prints the obsolete statement that ≥3 rungs license slope adjectives. Both were named in the approved remediation at [a5-llm-refute-adjudication.md](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/a5-llm-refute-adjudication.md:86).

Overall: the binding a5-llm and f2 corrections are statistically sound, properly authorized, and non-mutating. The remediation as a whole has residual defects: incomplete schema/freeze integration, an incorrect comparable-rung minimum below two rungs, an unfixed specialized generator, stale report language, and insufficient production-path regression coverage.
